title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR PROCESSING REAL-TIME VIDEO FROM A DEVICE FOR MEDICAL IMAGING AND OBJECT DETECTION IN VIDEO; SISTEMAS Y MÉTODOS PARA PROCESAR VÍDEO EN TIEMPO REAL DESDE UN DISPOSITIVO DE IMÁGENES MÉ- DICAS Y DETECCIÓN DE OBJETOS EN EL VÍDEO
abstract:
La presente se refiere a sistemas y métodos para procesar video en tiempo real y detectar objetos en el video. En una implementación, se provee un sistema que incluye un puerto de entrada para recibir video en tiempo real obtenido de un dispositivo de imágenes médicas, un primer bus para transferir el video en tiempo real recibido, y por lo menos un procesador configurado para recibir el video en tiempo real del primer bus, ejecutar la detección de objetos aplicando una red neuronal entrenada sobre los cuadros del video recibido en tiempo real, y superponer un borde que indica una ubicación de por lo menos un objeto detectado en los cuadros. El sistema además incluye un segundo bus para recibir el video con el borde superpuesto, un puerto de salida para emitir el video con el borde superpuesto del segundo bus a una pantalla externa, y un tercer bus para transmitir directamente el video en tiempo real recibido al puerto de salida.

This refers to systems and methods for processing video in real time and detecting objects in the video. In one implementation, a system is provided that includes an input port for receiving real-time video obtained from a medical imaging device, a first bus for transferring the received real-time video, and at least one processor configured to receive the real-time video of the first bus, execute object detection by applying a trained neural network on the frames of the video received in real time, and superimposing a border indicating a location of at least one detected object in the frames. The system also includes a second bus to receive the video with the overlapping edge, an output port to output the video with the overlapping edge of the second bus to an external display, and a third bus to directly transmit the video in real time received to the port of departure.

title: IMAGE ENHANCEMENT TECHNIQUES FOR AUTOMATED VISUAL INSPECTION; TÉCNICAS DE AUMENTO DE IMAGEN PARA INSPECCIÓN VISUAL AUTOMATIZADA
abstract:
Diversas técnicas facilitan el desarrollo de una biblioteca de imágenes que puede usarse para entrenar y/o validar un modelo de inspección visual automatizada (AVI), una red neuronal de AVI de este tipo para la clasificación de imágenes. En un aspecto, se usa un algoritmo de transposición aritmética para generar imágenes sintéticas a partir de imágenes originales transponiendo características (por ejemplo, defectos) a las imágenes originales, con realismo a nivel de píxel. En otros aspectos, se usan técnicas de restauración de imagen digital para generar imágenes sintéticas realistas a partir de imágenes originales. Pueden usarse técnicas de restauración de imagen basadas en aprendizaje profundo para añadir, eliminar y/o modificar defectos u otras características representadas. En otros aspectos adicionales, se usan técnicas de control de calidad para evaluar la idoneidad de las bibliotecas de imágenes para el entrenamiento y/o validación de modelos de AVI, y/o para evaluar si las imágenes individuales son adecuadas para su inclusión en tales bibliotecas.

Various techniques facilitate the development of an image library that can be used to train and/or validate an automated visual inspection (AVI) model, such an AVI neural network for image classification. In one aspect, an arithmetic transpose algorithm is used to generate synthetic images from original images by transposing features (eg, defects) to the original images, with realism at the pixel level. In other aspects, digital image restoration techniques are used to generate realistic synthetic images from original images. Deep learning-based image restoration techniques can be used to add, remove, and/or modify defects or other rendered features. In still other aspects, quality control techniques are used to assess the suitability of image libraries for AVI model training and/or validation, and/or to assess whether individual images are suitable for inclusion in such libraries. .

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR TRAINING GENERATIVE ANTAGONICAL NETWORKS AND USE OF TRAINED GENERATIVE ANTAGONICAL NETWORKS; SISTEMAS Y MÉTODOS PARA ENTRENAR REDES GENERATIVAS ANTAGÓNICAS Y USO DE REDES GENERATIVAS ANTAGÓNICAS ENTRENADAS
abstract:
La presente invención se refiere a sistemas y métodos implementados por computadora para el entrenamiento y uso de redes generativas antagónicas. En una implementación, un sistema para entrenar una red generativa antagónica puede incluir al menos un procesador que puede proveer una pluralidad de imágenes que incluyen representaciones de una característica de interés e indicadores de ubicación de la característica de interés y el uso de la primera pluralidad de indicadores para entrenar una red de detección de objetos. Además, el o los procesadores pueden proveer una segunda pluralidad de imágenes que incluye representaciones de la característica de interés, y aplicar la red de detección de objetos entrenada a la segunda pluralidad para producir una pluralidad de detecciones de la característica de interés. Adicionalmente, el o los procesadores pueden proveer verificaciones establecidas manualmente de verdaderos positivos y falsos positivos con respecto a la pluralidad de detecciones, usar las verificaciones para entrenar una red generativa antagónica, y volver a entrenar la red generativa antagónica usando por lo menos un conjunto adicional de imágenes, detecciones adicionales y otras verificaciones establecidas manualmente.

The present invention relates to computer-implemented systems and methods for the training and use of generative adversarial networks. In one implementation, a system for training a generative adversarial network may include at least one processor that may provide a plurality of images that include representations of a feature of interest and location indicators of the feature of interest and use of the first plurality of indicators to train an object detection network. Additionally, the processor(s) may provide a second plurality of images that include representations of the feature of interest, and apply the trained object detection network to the second plurality to produce a plurality of detections of the feature of interest. Additionally, the processor(s) may provide manually set checks of true positives and false positives with respect to the plurality of detections, use the checks to train a generative adversarial network, and retrain the generative adversarial network using at least one additional set. of images, additional detections and other verifications set manually.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR FAULT DETECTION FOR RAILWAY VEHICLE CABLE TERMINALS; MÉTODO Y APARATO DE DETECCIÓN DE FALLAS PARA TERMINALES DE CABLES DE VEHÍCULOS FERROVIARIOS
abstract:
La presente solicitud se refiere al campo del tránsito ferroviario, y en particular a un método y un aparato de detección de fallas de un terminal de cable de un vehículo ferroviario. El método para la detección de fallas incluye recopilar datos de temperatura ambiente y datos de análisis de un terminal de cable a detectar; y analizar y procesar los datos de temperatura ambiente y los datos de análisis utilizando un modelo de detección preestablecido para obtener un resultado de detección del terminal de cable a detectar; donde los datos de análisis comprenden datos de imágenes, datos de temperatura y datos sonoros; y el modelo de detección preestablecido incluye un modelo de detección para analizar y procesar los datos de temperatura ambiente, los datos de las imágenes y los datos de temperatura, o un modelo de detección para analizar y procesar los datos de temperatura ambiente, los datos de imágenes los datos de temperatura y los datos sonoros. La presente solicitud resuelve el problema del estado de la técnica, en la que el método de detección existente suele perder información o a entregar información errónea en la detección de los terminales de cable, dando como resultado inexactitud en la detección de fallas y averías de terminales de cable de los vehículos ferroviarios.

The present application relates to the field of rail transit, and in particular to a method and apparatus for detecting faults in a cable terminal of a railway vehicle. The method for fault detection includes collecting ambient temperature data and analysis data of a cable terminal to be detected; and analyzing and processing the ambient temperature data and the analysis data using a preset detection model to obtain a detection result of the cable terminal to be detected; where the analysis data comprises image data, temperature data and sound data; and the preset detection model includes a detection model for analyzing and processing ambient temperature data, image data, and temperature data, or a detection model for analyzing and processing ambient temperature data, image data, temperature data, and sound data. The present application solves the problem of the state of the art, in which the existing detection method often loses information or delivers erroneous information in the detection of cable terminals, resulting in inaccuracy in the detection of faults and breakdowns of cable terminals of railway vehicles.
claims:

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR TRAINING A GENERATIVE ADVERSE NETWORK USING IMAGES THAT INCLUDE REPRESENTATIONS OF AN ANOMALY; SISTEMA Y MÉTODO PARA ENTRENAR UNA RED GENERATIVA ANTAGÓNICA USANDO IMÁGENES QUE INCLUYEN REPRESENTACIONES DE UNA ANOMALÍA
abstract:
La presente invención se refiere a sistemas y métodos implementados por computadora para el entrenamiento y uso de redes generativas antagónicas. En una implementación, un sistema para entrenar una red generativa antagónica puede incluir al menos un procesador que puede proveer una pluralidad de imágenes que incluyen representaciones de una característica de interés e indicadores de ubicación de la característica de interés y el uso de la primera pluralidad de indicadores para entrenar una red de detección de objetos. Además, el o los procesadores pueden proveer una segunda pluralidad de imágenes que incluye representaciones de la característica de interés, y aplicar la red de detección de objetos entrenada a la segunda pluralidad para producir una pluralidad de detecciones de la característica de interés. Adicionalmente, el o los procesadores pueden proveer verificaciones establecidas manualmente de verdaderos positivos y falsos positivos con respecto a la pluralidad de detecciones, usar las verificaciones para entrenar una red generativa antagónica, y volver a entrenar la red generativa antagónica usando por lo menos un conjunto adicional de imágenes, detecciones adicionales y otras verificaciones establecidas manualmente.

The present invention relates to computer-implemented systems and methods for training and using generative adversarial networks. In one implementation, a system for training a generative adversarial network may include at least one processor that can provide a plurality of images that include representations of a feature of interest and location indicators of the feature of interest and using the first plurality of indicators to train an object detection network. In addition, the processor(s) may provide a second plurality of images that include representations of the feature of interest, and apply the trained object detection network to the second plurality to produce a plurality of detections of the feature of interest. Additionally, the processor(s) may provide manually established checks for true positives and false positives with respect to the plurality of detections, use the checks to train a generative adversarial network, and retrain the generative adversarial network using at least one additional set of images, additional detections, and other manually established checks.
claims:

title: LIQUID PETROLEUM GAS (LPG) BOTTLE DISTRIBUTING MACHINE; MÁQUINA EXPENDEDORA DE GARRAFAS DE GAS LICUADO DE PETRÓLEO GLP
abstract:
La máquina expendedora objeto de la presente invención, dispone de un recinto ventilado que evita la acumulación de gas ante fugas eventuales, sensores de control y mecanismos de corte automático de alimentación de energía y funcionamiento. Dicha máquina expendedora de garrafas permite la recepción de envases vacíos por parte del usuario, a la vez que comprende medios para detectar la entrega de un envase correcto y evitar falsificaciones o maniobras fraudulentas, y comprende medios de conexión a redes de telecomunicación que permitan proveer información acerca del funcionamiento de las máquinas, el estado operativo, la disponibilidad de stock de garrafas, entre otros parámetros.

The vending machine object of the present invention has a ventilated enclosure that prevents the accumulation of gas in the event of leaks, control sensors and mechanisms for automatically cutting off the power supply and operation. This vending machine for gas bottles allows the user to receive empty containers, while also including means to detect the delivery of a correct container and prevent counterfeiting or fraudulent maneuvers. It also includes means for connecting to telecommunications networks that allow information to be provided about the operation of the machines, the operating status, the availability of gas bottles in stock, among other parameters.

title: DYE PENETRANT METHOD FOR NON-DESTRUCTIVE TESTING OF A TEST SPECIMEN AND TEST DEVICE; FARBEINDRINGVERFAHREN ZUR ZERSTÖRUNGSFREIEN PRÜFUNG EINES PRÜFLINGS UND PRÜFVORRICHTUNG
abstract:
Die Erfindung betrifft ein Farbeindringverfahren zur zerstörungsfreien Prüfung eines Prüflings (12), mit den Schritten Bestrahlen des mit einem Fluoreszenzfarbstoff (30) versehenen Prüflings (12) mit Anregungs-Photonen (20), sodass Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28) entstehen, und Erfassen von Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28), mit den Schritten Erfassen einer Verzögerungszeit (t) der Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28) zwischen dem Auftreffen der Anregungs-Photonen (20) auf dem Prüfling (12) und der Emission des Fluoreszenz-Photons (28.1) und Ermitteln von Oberflächenfehlern (32) des Prüflings (12) anhand der Verzögerungszeiten (t).

The invention relates to a dye penetration method for the non-destructive testing of a test specimen (12), comprising the steps of irradiating the test specimen (12) provided with a fluorescent dye (30) with excitation photons (20) so that fluorescence photons (28) are generated, and detecting fluorescence photons (28), with the steps of detecting a delay time (t) of the fluorescence photons (28) between the impact of the excitation photons (20) on the test piece (12) and the emission of the fluorescence photon (28.1) and determining surface defects (32) of the test piece (12) based on the delay times (t).
claims:
' Gramm Lins intellectual property Becker & Hickl GmbH Anwaltsakte: Nunsdorfer Ring 7-9 6458-0005 DE-1 12277 Berlin Datum: 11. September 2023 Patentansprüche 1. Farbeindringverfahren zur zerstörungsfreien Prüfung eines Prüflings (12), mit den Schritten (a) Bestrahlen des mit einem Fluoreszenzfarbstoff (30) versehenen Prüflings (12) mit Anregungs-Photonen (20), sodass Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28) entstehen, und (b) Erfassen von Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28), gekennzeichnet durch die Schritte (c) Erfassen einer Verzögerungszeit (t) der Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28) zwischen dem Auftreffen der Anregungs-Photonen (20) auf dem Prüfling (12) und der Emission des Fluoreszenz-Photons (28.1) und (d) Ermitteln von Oberflächenfehlern (32) des Prüflings (12) anhand der Verzögerungszeiten (t). 2. Farbeindringverfahren nach Anspruch 1, gekennzeichnet durch die Schritte (a) Aufbringen eines Fluoreszenzfarbstoffs (30) auf den Prüfling (12) und (b) danach und vor dem Bestrahlen des Prüflings (12) mit AnregungsPhotonen (20) Entfernen eines Teils des Fluoreszenzfarbstoffs (30). 21/30 PL/PL - PD002 / 20.10 3. Verfahren nach einem der vorstehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass das Ermitteln von Oberflächenfehlern (32) des Prüflings (12) anhand der Verzögerungszeiten (t) die folgenden Schritte umfasst: (a) sukzessives Erfassen der Verzögerungszeiten (t) der FluoreszenzPhotonen (28) für alle Punkte eines Messbereichs auf der Oberfläche (O) des Prüflings (12) und (b) Ausgeben einer Warnmeldung, wenn ein Oberflächenfehler (32) ermittelt wurde und/oder die folgenden Schritte umfasst: (a) ortsaufgelöstes Erfassen einer Verzögerungszeit (t) der FluoreszenzPhotonen (28), sodass eine verzögerungszeitabhängige Fluoreszenzphotonenanzahl (n) erhalten wird und (b) Darstellen nur solcher Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28), deren Verzögerungszeiten (t) in einem vorgegebenen Akzeptanzintervall (I) liegt, in einem Bild (B) und/oder Darstellen eines Bildes (B), das ein Verhältnis der Fluoreszenzphotonenanzahlen zweier unterschiedlicher Verzögerungszeiten (t) und/oder unterschiedlicher Spektralbereiche zeigt. 4. Verfahren nach einem der vorstehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass das Ermitteln von Oberflächenfehlern (32) des Prüflings (12) anhand der Verzögerungszeiten (t) die folgenden Schritte umfasst: (a) Anpassen der verzögerungszeitabhängigen Fluoreszenzphotonenanzahl (n(t)) mit (i) einer ersten Abklingfunktion (fi), die die Abhängigkeit (n(t)) der pro Zeiteinheit emittierten Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28) in Abhängigkeit von einer ersten Abklingkonstanten (τι) und einem ersten, insbesondere ortsaufgelösten, Vorfaktor (no,i) angibt, und (ii) zumindest einer zweiten Abklingfunktion, die die Abhängigkeit der pro Zeiteinheit emittierten Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28) in Abhängigkeit von einer zweiten Abklingkonstante (τς), die sich von der ersten 22/30 -3Abklingkonstante (τ-ι) unterscheidet, und einem zweiten, insbesondere ortsaufgelösten, Vorfaktor (no,2) angibt, und (b) Ermitteln des Oberflächenfehlers (32) aus einem der ortsaufgelösten Vorfaktoren (no,i, no,2) und/oder Darstellen der ortsaufgelösten Vorfaktoren (no,i, no,2) in einem Bild (30). 5. Verfahren nach einem der vorstehenden Ansprüche, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass (a) das Akzeptanzintervall (I) eine untere Grenze in Form einer MinimalVerzögerungszeit (tmin) hat, die größer ist als die Flugzeit am Prüfling (12) reflektierter oder gestreuter Photonen und/oder Verfahren nach einem der vorstehenden Ansprüche und/oder (b) die Minimal-Verzögerungszeit größer als die Abklingzeit von Fluoreszenzstrahlung des Materials, aus dem der Prüfling (12) besteht, und/oder von am Prüfling (12) haftendem Schmutz. 6. Prüfvorrichtung (10) zur zerstörungsfreien Prüfung von Prüflingen (12), insbesondere auf Oberflächen- oder Randzonenfehler (32), mit (a) einer Aufnahme (16) zum Aufnehmen des Prüflings (12), (b) einer Lichtquelle (18), insbesondere einem Laser, zum Bestrahlen des Prüflings (12) mit Anregungs-Photonen (20), (c) einem Detektor (24), insbesondere einer Kamera, zum Erfassen von Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28), die vom Prüfling (12) aufgrund der Bestrahlung abgegeben werden, (d) einer Auswerteeinheit (34), die eingerichtet ist zum automatischen (i) Erfassen einer Verzögerungszeit (t) der Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28) zwischen dem Auftreffen der Anregungs-Photonen (20) auf dem Prüfling (12) und der Emission des Fluoreszenz-Photons (28.1) und (ii) Darstellen eines Bilds (B) des Prüflings (12) anhand der von Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28), deren Verzögerungszeiten (t) in einem vorgegebenen Akzeptanzintervall (I) liegt und/oder (iii) Berechnen zumindest eines Fehlerparameters (P), der ein Ausmaß von Oberflächenfehlern (32) des Prüflings (12) kodiert, aus den selektierten Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28). 23/30 7. Prüfvorrichtung (10) nach Anspruch 6, gekennzeichnet durch (a) eine Aufbringvorrichtung zum Aufbringen eines Fluoreszenzfarbstoffs (30) auf den Prüfling (12) und (b) eine Entfernvorrichtung zum Entfernen eines Teils des Fluoreszenzfarbstoffs (30). 8. Prüfvorrichtung (10) nach einem der Ansprüche 6 oder 7, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass (a) die Aufnahme zum Befestigen am Prüfling ausgebildet ist, (b) die Prüfvorrichtung einen Antrieb (66) zum Bewegen der Lichtquelle (18) und/oder des Detektors (24) und/oder eines Messkopfs (68) relativ zum Prüfling (12) aufweist, und (c) zum selbstständigen Bewegen, insbesondere entlang des Prüflings (12), ausgebildet ist. 9. Prüfvorrichtung (10) nach einem der Ansprüche 6 bis 8, gekennzeichnet durch (a) einen Helligkeitssensor (70) zum Erfassen einer Umgebungshelligkeit, (b) wobei die Auswerteeinheit (34) eingerichtet ist zum automatischen (i) zeitabhängiges Erfassen der Umgebungshelligkeit, (ii) Ermitteln einer periodischen Helligkeitsänderung und (iii) Ansteuern der Lichtquelle (18) und/oder des Detektors (24), sodass nur Fluoreszenz-Photonen (28) erfasst werden, deren die Anregungs-Photonen (20) in einem Minderhelligkeits-Zeitintervall, in dem die Umgebungshelligkeit kleiner ist als eine mittlere Umgebungshelligkeit, auf den Prüfling aufgetroffen sind. 10. Herstellvorrichtung (44) zum Herstellen eines Produkts (56) aus zumindest zwei Komponenten, mit (a) einer Anlage (46) zum Herstellen der ersten Komponente (), (b) einer Prüfvorrichtung (10) nach einem der Ansprüche 6 bis 7, die angeordnet und ausgebildet ist zum, insbesondere automatischen, Prüfen dieser Komponente () und (c) ggf. einer Montageanlage (54) zum Montieren des Produkts (56) unter Verwendung der geprüften Komponente (). 24/30 11. Herstellvorrichtung (44) nach Anspruch 10, dadurch gekennzeichnet, dass sie eine Lager-Herstellvorrichtung zum Herstellen eines Lagers, insbesondere eines Wälzlagers (56) aus zumindest zwei Wälzlager-Bestandteilen, insbesondere zumindest einem Wälzkörper. 12. Verwendung eines Fluoreszenzlebensdauer-Mikroskops oder Fluoreszenzlebensdauer-Messgeräts zur zerstörungsfreien Prüfung auf Oberflächenfehler (32), insbesondere Risse, in Oberflächen (O) von Bauteilen . 25/30

'Gram Lin's intellectual property Becker & Hickl GmbH lawyer file: Nunsdorfer Ring 7-9 6458-0005 DE-1 12277 Berlin Date: September 11, 2023 Patent claims 1. Dye penetration method for the non-destructive testing of a test specimen (12), comprising the steps of (a) irradiating the test specimen (12) provided with a fluorescent dye (30) with excitation photons (20) so that fluorescence photons (28) are generated, and (b) detecting fluorescence photons (28), characterized by the steps (c) detecting a delay time (t) of the fluorescence photons (28) between the incidence of the excitation photons (20) on the test piece (12) and the emission of the fluorescence photon (28.1) and (d) determining surface defects (32) of the test piece (12) based on the delay times (t). 2. Dye penetration method according to claim 1, characterized by the steps (a) applying a fluorescent dye (30) to the test piece (12) and (b) thereafter and before irradiating the test piece (12) with excitation photons (20) removing a part of the fluorescent dye (30). 21/30 PL/PL - PD002 / 20.10 3. Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the determination of surface defects (32) of the test piece (12) based on the delay times (t) comprises the following steps: (a) successively detecting the delay times (t) of the fluorescence photons (28) for all points of a measuring area on the surface (O) of the test piece (12) and (b) issuing a warning message if a surface defect (32) has been detected and/or comprises the following steps: (a) spatially resolved detection of a delay time (t) of the fluorescence photons (28), so that a delay-time-dependent number of fluorescence photons (n) is obtained and (b) displaying only those fluorescence photons (28) whose delay times (t) lie in a predetermined acceptance interval (I) in an image (B) and/or Displaying an image (B) showing a ratio of the fluorescence photon numbers of two different delay times (t) and/or different spectral ranges. 4. Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the determination of surface defects (32) of the test piece (12) based on the delay times (t) comprises the following steps: (a) adjusting the delay-time-dependent number of fluorescence photons (n(t)) with (i) a first decay function (fi) which indicates the dependence (n(t)) of the fluorescence photons (28) emitted per unit time as a function of a first decay constant (τι) and a first, in particular spatially resolved, prefactor (no,i), and (ii) at least one second decay function, which shows the dependence of the fluorescence photons (28) emitted per unit time on a second decay constant (τς) which differs from the first 22/30 -3decay constant (τ-ι), and a second, in particular spatially resolved, prefactor (no,2), and (b) determining the surface error (32) from one of the spatially resolved prefactors (no,i, no,2) and/or Displaying the spatially resolved prefactors (no,i, no,2) in an image (30). 5. Method according to one of the preceding claims, characterized in that (a) the acceptance interval (I) has a lower limit in the form of a minimum delay time (tmin) which is greater than the flight time of photons reflected or scattered at the test object (12) and/or method according to one of the preceding claims and/or (b) the minimum delay time is greater than the decay time of fluorescent radiation of the material from which the test object (12) consists, and/or dirt adhering to the test specimen (12). 6. Testing device (10) for the non-destructive testing of test pieces (12), in particular for surface or edge zone defects (32), with (a) a holder (16) for holding the test piece (12), (b) a light source (18), in particular a laser, for irradiating the test piece (12) with excitation photons (20), (c) a detector (24), in particular a camera, for detecting fluorescence photons (28) emitted by the test piece (12) due to the irradiation, (d) an evaluation unit (34), which is configured to automatically (i) detect a delay time (t) of the fluorescence photons (28) between the incidence of the excitation photons (20) on the test object (12) and the emission of the fluorescence photon (28.1) and (ii) display an image (B) of the test object (12) based on the fluorescence photons (28) whose delay times (t) lie within a predetermined acceptance interval (I) and/or (iii) calculate at least one error parameter (P), which encodes an extent of surface defects (32) of the test specimen (12), from the selected fluorescence photons (28). 23/30 7. Test device (10) according to claim 6, characterized by (a) an application device for applying a fluorescent dye (30) to the test piece (12) and (b) a removal device for removing a part of the fluorescent dye (30). 8. Testing device (10) according to one of claims 6 or 7, characterized in that (a) the receptacle is designed for fastening to the test object, (b) the testing device has a drive (66) for moving the light source (18) and/or the detector (24) and/or a measuring head (68) relative to the test object (12), and (c) is designed for independent movement, in particular along the test object (12). 9. Testing device (10) according to one of claims 6 to 8, characterized by (a) a brightness sensor (70) for detecting an ambient brightness, (b) wherein the evaluation unit (34) is configured for automatically (i) time-dependent detection of the ambient brightness, (ii) determining a periodic brightness change and (iii) controlling the light source (18) and/or the detector (24) so ​​that only fluorescence photons (28) are detected, whose excitation photons (20) hit the test object in a low-brightness time interval in which the ambient brightness is less than an average ambient brightness. 10. Manufacturing device (44) for manufacturing a product (56) from at least two components, with (a) a system (46) for manufacturing the first component (), (b) a testing device (10) according to one of claims 6 to 7, which is arranged and designed for, in particular automatically, testing of this component () and (c) optionally an assembly system (54) for assembling the product (56) using the tested component (). 24/30 11. title: APPARATUS FOR AND METHOD OF INSPECTING SURFACE TOPOGRAPHY OF A MOVING OBJECT
abstract: A dynamic photometric stereo inspection technique usable to capture and analyse the topography of a moving surface. The technique includes an enhanced data capture method and apparatus comprising a spaced array of at least two coplanar illuminates to improve measurement range and accuracy. The apparatus can be used to inspect banknotes, e.g. to assist with fitness assessment and/or forgery detection. The method may comprise automatically assessing surface topography data to provide qualitative and quantitative information about 2D and 3D features, such as changes in reflectivity, colour, glossiness, 3D texture and the surface profile of the surface under inspection.
claims: CLAIMS 1. A method for inspecting a surface, the method comprising: illuminating a surface with three or more inspection beams, each inspection beam being output from a respective illuminate, the illuminates being spaced from each other over the surface; obtaining a plurality of digital images of the surface from a digital image capturing device; and calculating a magnitude and a direction for a surface normal component at each of a plurality of inspection points on the surface based on the plurality of digital images and a predetermined incident light vector from each of the illuminates at each inspection point, wherein the illuminates are arranged relative to the surface so that their predetermined incident light vectors are coplanar at each inspection point. 2. A method according to claim 1 including moving the surface relative to the digital image capturing device while the plurality of digital images is obtained. 3. A method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein calculating the magnitude and direction for the surface normal component at each inspection point comprises applying a surface reflectance lighting model to a detected light intensity at each inspection point obtained and the predetermined incident light vectors from the illuminates at each inspection point. 4. A method according to claim 3, wherein the lighting model is a Lambertian reflection model or a specular reflection model. 5. A method according to any preceding claim including: generating inspection data from the magnitude and direction of the surface normal components of the inspection points; and analysing the inspection data to identify properties of the surface. WO 2015/185935 PCT/GB2015/051639 6. A method according to claim 5, wherein analysing the inspection data comprising comparing the behaviour of the surface normal components across the surface with characteristic surface normal behaviour associated with one or more surface defects. 7. A method according to claim 6, wherein the one or more surface defects include tearing, folding, soiling and crumpling. 8. A method according to any one of claims 5 to 7, wherein analysing the inspection data comprises determining specular properties of the surface. 9. A method according to claim 8, wherein determining specular properties of the surface includes detecting the presence of a transparent layer mounted on the surface. 10. A method according to any one of claims 5 to 9, wherein the inspection data comprises one or more of: a bump map comprising a dense array of the surface normal component directions calculated for the plurality of inspection points; an albedo comprising a map of the surface normal component magnitudes calculated for the plurality of inspection points . 11. A method according to claim 10, wherein the inspection data further includes any one or more of: a shadow pattern obtained from one or more of the plurality of digital images; a full colour image of the surface. 12. A method according to any preceding claim, wherein illuminating the surface includes multiplexing the inspection beams in any of a temporal, spatial or spectral sense. 13. An apparatus for inspecting a surface, the apparatus comprising: WO 2015/185935 PCT/GB2015/051639 three or more illuminates mounted in a spaced arrangement over an inspection plane, each of the three or more illuminates being arranged to output an inspection beam to illuminate a region of the inspection plane; a digital image capturing device having the inspection plane in its field of view; and a processor arranged to receive a plurality of digital images from the digital image capturing device, each of the plurality of digital images including an image of the inspection plane when illuminated by an inspection beam from a respective illuminate, wherein the processor is arranged to calculate a magnitude and a direction for a surface normal component at each of a plurality of inspection points on the inspection plane based on the plurality of digital images and a predetermined incident light vector from each of the illuminates at each inspection point, and wherein the three or more illuminates are arranged so that their predetermined incident light vectors are coplanar at each inspection point. 14. An apparatus according to claim 13 or 14, wherein the processor is arranged to calculate the magnitude and direction of the surface normal component by applying a surface reflectance lighting model to a detected light intensity at each inspection point obtained from the plurality of digital images and the predetermined incident light vectors from the illuminates at each inspection point. 16. An apparatus according to claim 15, wherein the processor is arranged to generate inspection data from the magnitude and direction of the surface normal components of the inspection points, and analyse the inspection data to identify properties of the surface. WO 2015/185935 PCT/GB2015/051639 17. An apparatus according to any one of claims 13 to 16, wherein each of the three or more illuminates comprises a line light for outputting a planar light beam that intersects with the inspection plane along an inspection line. 18. An apparatus according to claim 17, wherein the three or more illuminates output illumination in different discrete wavelength bands, and wherein the planar light beams from the three or more illuminates intersect with the inspection plane along a common inspection line. 19. An apparatus according to claim 17 or 18, wherein the inspection lines of the three or more illuminates lie adjacent one another on the inspection plane. 20. An apparatus for inspecting a surface, the apparatus comprising: an illuminate mounted adjacent to a predefined travel path for a inspection surface, the illuminate being arranged WO 2015/185935 PCT/GB2015/051639 to output an inspection beam to illuminate a region of the inspection surface as it moves relative to the illuminate; a digital image capturing device having the illuminated region of the inspection surface in its field of view; and a processor arranged to receive a plurality of timespaced digital images from the digital image capturing device, wherein the region of the inspection surface in the field of view of the digital image capturing device is curved; wherein the processor is arranged to calculate a magnitude and a direction for a surface normal component at each of a plurality of inspection points on the inspection surface based on the time-spaced plurality of digital images and a set of predetermined incident light vectors from the illuminate at each inspection point in each of the time-spaced plurality of digital images, and wherein the set of predetermined incident light vectors for each inspection point are coplanar. 25. An inspection apparatus for banknotes, comprising: a feed mechanism for conveying a banknote across an inspection plane; and a photometric stereo measurement system arranged to detect a surface topography of the banknote as it passes across the inspection plane; and an analysis processor arranged to identify defects in the banknote from the surface topography. 26. WO 2015/185935 PCT/GB2015/051639 33. A method of analysing surface topography of a moving surface, the method comprising; obtaining a bump map comprising a dense array of surface normal component directions for a plurality of inspection points on a surface; modelling the behaviour of the surface normal component directions in a region of the surface; identifying a property of the surface based on the modelled behaviour. 34. A method of analysing surface topography according to any one of claims 33 to 41 including using the identified property of the surface in a process control method. 43. A method of analysing surface topography according 25 to claim 33 to 42, wherein the surface is the surface of a banknote, and wherein the method includes using the identified property of the surface to determine the fitness of the banknote . title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR SAMPLE DISPLAY AND REVIEW
abstract: Methods and systems for displaying images of cells in a sample include obtaining a plurality of images of cells in the sample, where each image corresponds to one of the cells in the sample, determining values of at least one property for each of the cells based on the plurality of images, arranging the plurality of images to form a first image array, where the images are ordered in the first image array based on the values of the at least one property, displaying the first image array, sorting the plurality of images to form a second image array in which an ordering of the images is different from the first image array, and displaying the second image array, where the sample includes blood and the cells include red blood cells. 7243495_1 (GHMatters) P93393.AU.1 KAROLA
claims: The claims defining the invention are as follows: 1. A method of displaying images of cells in a sample, the method comprising: determining values of a property for each one of a set of cells; displaying a set of images of the cells in an ordered array on a display device, wherein each image in the set of images corresponds to only one of the cells in the set of cells and is ordered in the array based on the value of the property for the one cell; and displaying a graphical representation of a distribution of the values of the property on the display device. 2. A method according to claim 1, further comprising: displaying each one of the set of images as a user-selectable control on the display device; and monitoring the display device to determine when one of the set of images is selected. 3. A method according to claim 2, further comprising, when one of the set of images is selected, displaying a visual marker on the graphical representation of the distribution of values, wherein a position of the visual marker on the graphical representation is based on the value of the property for the cell corresponding to the selected image. 4. A method according to claim 3, further comprising displaying a visual marker in the ordered array, wherein a position of the visual marker in the ordered array is based on the selected image. 25 5. A method according to claim 4, wherein the visual marker in the ordered array comprises an element that surrounds the selected image. 6. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the graphical representation comprises a histogram. 9201726_1 (GHMatters) G63612 2015271917 23 Jun2017 7. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 6, further comprising displaying an expected distribution of values of the property on the graphical representation of the distribution. 8. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 7, further comprising displaying one or more visual markers corresponding to at least one of upper and lower threshold values of the property on the graphical representation of the distribution, wherein the upper and lower threshold values define a range of values of the property that are expected for a patient. 9. A method according to claim 8, further comprising displaying one or more visual markers corresponding to at least one of the upper and lower threshold values in the ordered array. 10. A method according to claim 9, further comprising displaying the images of the set that correspond to cells having a value of the property that is less than the lower threshold value or larger than the upper threshold value in a highlighted display modality in the ordered array. 11. A method according to any one of claims 2 to 10, further comprising, when one of the set of images is selected, displaying the value of the property for the cell corresponding to the selected image on the display device. 12. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 11, further comprising: obtaining one or more images of the set of cells; and determining the values of the property based on the obtained one or more images. 13. title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR SAMPLE DISPLAY AND REVIEW
abstract: Methods and systems for displaying images of cells in a sample include obtaining a plurality of images of cells in the sample, where each image corresponds to one of the cells in the sample, determining values of at least one property for each of the cells based on the plurality of images, arranging the plurality of images to form a first image array, where the images are ordered in the first image array based on the values of the at least one property, displaying the first image array, sorting the plurality of images to form a second image array in which an ordering of the images is different from the first image array, and displaying the second image array, where the sample includes blood and the cells include red blood cells. 7243495_1 (GHMatters) P93393.AU.1 KAROLA
claims: WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. The method of claim 9, further comprising analyzing the plurality of images to identify inclusions in the cells, wherein the second property is a property related to the inclusions. 17. title: STEPWISE-REFINEMENT PAVEMENT CRACK DETECTION METHOD
abstract: Disclosed is a stepwise-refinement pavement crack detection method. A processing on a pavement image comprises the following main steps: a road mark extraction and processing, an ROA extraction, an ROA-based adaptive segmentation by thresholds, an ROC extraction and an ROC directional feature weighting-based region growth. On the basis that a crack is a collection of pixel points having similar gray scales and has a significant spatial aggregation feature, the present invention can quickly extract an ROA, quickly position, via the ROA, a possible gray-scale section and a spatial position of a crack, adaptively segment an image by thresholds, establish a confidence evaluation criteria, and accurately extract an ROC. A crack growing direction estimation method based on segmental weighting can accurately estimate a growing trend of a crack. The present invention employs an improved region growth method, and a crack utilizes a confidence region as a seed region and grows with the growing trend of the seed region, thus fully ensuring an accuracy of crack growth and a completeness of crack detection.
claims: 2015392660 02 Apr 2019 The claims defining the invention are as follows: 1. A stepwise refinement detection method for pavement cracks, comprising collecting pavement images, analyzing the pavement images, wherein analyzing the pavement images comprises the following steps: extracting suspected crack regions of accumulation ROA, comprising: extracting the connected regions with certain grayscale features in the pavement images, screening the connected regions according to certain preset conditions to obtain the suspected crack regions of accumulation, where the preset conditions are prior obtained according to the geometric features of the crack regions; extracting crack regions of confidence ROC, comprising: for screened ROA regions, establishing a target determination system to screen out initial crack confidence regions with a certain degree of crack confidence by the geometry, spatial location and grayscale statistic features of ROA regions; for the initial crack confidence regions, connecting and merging the regions with close locations and similarities according to the spatial locations and regional similarities between the initial crack confidence regions, so as to form enhanced crack confidence regions, i.e. the crack regions of confidence ROC. determining region growth based on ROC direction feature weighting, the determination comprising: step 4-1. extracting feature vectors of the directions and lengths of all ROCs; step 4-2. calculating search range from the longest ROC; 2015392660 02 Apr 2019 step 4-3. searching all growable regions within the range; step 4-4. merging ROCs and the growable regions: for all regions in the growable region set, merging the regions with close locations sequentially according to the spatial location relation between ROCs and the growable regions, then terminating the current ROC growth; step 4-5. determining the composition of cracks: if the current ROC growth stops, determining whether the crack conditions are satisfied according to the total length Rgrowlen of the grown regions; if satisfied, proceeding to step 4-6, otherwise the region growth is completed; step 4-6. extending the next ROC: repeating the step 4-2 to step 4-5 from the next ROC till the growths of all ROCs are completed, then the region growth is completed, and, before extracting the crack regions of confidence ROC, segmenting the images by adaptive threshold based on ROA, comprising: dividing the images into non-overlapping sub-block images; the sub-block images comprising: sub-block image I containing the suspected crack regions of accumulation, sub-block image II which is adjacent to the sub-block image I in location and does not contain the suspected crack regions of accumulation, and sub-block image III which does not contain the suspected crack regions of accumulation and is not adjacent to the sub-block image I; segmenting the sub-block images I, II and III according to different thresholds respectively; wherein, the segmentation threshold for the sub-block image I is determined according to the grayscale feature of the ROA, the segmentation threshold for the sub-block image II is determined by referring to the adjacent sub-block image I; title: ATOMIC THERAPEUTIC INDICATOR; INDICATEUR THÉRAPEUTIQUE ATOMIQUE
abstract:
The present invention relates to the generation of an Atomic Therapeutic Indicator (ATI) for a test sample by the quantification of manganese; in voxels of a 3D region of the sample, wherein the 3D region is topographically defined by co-ordinates X'xY'xZ. The ATI is used to assess the radio-responsiveness i.e. sensitivity or resistance to radiation treatment, of a cancer i.e. a tumour/neoplasm. In a preferred embodiment, the present invention relates to a method of generating the ATI, assessing the radio- responsiveness of a tumour/neoplasm based on the ATI and, based on the assessment, either treating or not treating the tumour with radiation. The present invention also relates to a method of determining if a cancer is likely to reoccur post radiation treatment comprising quantifying the level of manganese in voxels of a 3D region of a test sample from the cancer and determining the frequency of high metallomic regions (HMRs) in the cancer, wherein a high frequency of HMRs is indicative that the cancer is likely to reoccur and a low frequency of HMRs is indicative that the cancer is unlikely to reoccur; and associated methods of treatment.

La présente invention concerne la génération d'un Indicateur Thérapeutique Atomique (ATI) pour un échantillon d'essai par quantification de manganèse, dans des voxels d'une région 3D de l'échantillon, la région 3D étant topographiquement définie par des coordonnées X'xY'xZ. L'ATI est utilisé pour évaluer la radio-réceptivité, c'est-à-dire la sensibilité ou la résistance à un traitement par rayonnement, d'un cancer, c'est-à-dire une tumeur/un néoplasme. Dans un mode de réalisation préféré, la présente invention concerne un procédé consistant à générer l'ATI, évaluer la radio-réceptivité d'une tumeur/d'un néoplasme sur la base de l'ATI et, en se basant sur l'évaluation, traiter ou non la tumeur avec un rayonnement. La présente invention concerne également un procédé consistant à déterminer si un cancer est susceptible de réapparaître après le traitement par rayonnement, comprenant la quantification du niveau de manganèse dans des voxels d'une région 3D d'un échantillon d'essai du cancer et la détermination de la fréquence de régions hautement metallomiques (HMR) dans le cancer, une fréquence élevée de HMR indiquant que le cancer est susceptible de réapparaître et une basse fréquence de HMR indiquant que le cancer est peu susceptible de réapparaître. L'invention concerne également des procédés associés de traitement.
claims: CLAIMS: 1 . A method of generating an Atomic Therapeutic Indicator (ATI) of a biological test sample comprising quantifying the level of manganese in voxels in a 3D region of the test sample, wherein the method comprises: (a) selecting a 2D region of said test sample, wherein the 2D region is topographically defined by an Χ'Ύ' coordinate system wherein X' is the length of the 2D region and Y' is the breadth of the 2D region, wherein the 3D region corresponds to said 2D region and has a selected height represented by Z, wherein the 3D region is divided into voxels of a pre-defined volume, the volume of each voxel being defined by XxYxZ wherein X is the length of the voxel, Y is the breadth of the voxel and Z is the height of the voxel; (b) quantifying the level of manganese in each voxel; and(c) calculating the central tendency level of manganese in selected voxels; wherein the central tendency level of manganese in the selected voxels defines the ATI. 2. A method of determining the radio-responsiveness of a cancer, the method comprising generating an ATI of a test sample according to claim 1 , wherein the lower the ATI the more sensitive the cancer is to radiation and the higher the ATI the more resistant the cancer is to radiation. 3. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein quantification of the level of manganese in the voxels of the test sample is calibrated using a reference standard, wherein the reference standard comprises one or more reference voxels, and wherein each reference voxel comprises a known quantity of manganese. 4. The method according to claim 3, wherein reference standard is a biological sample comprising a known quantity of endogenous or exogenous manganese. 5. title: ATOMIC THERAPEUTIC INDICATOR
abstract: The present invention relates to the generation of an Atomic Therapeutic Indicator (ATI) for a test sample by the quantification of manganese; in voxels of a 3D region of the sample, wherein the 3D region is topographically defined by co-ordinates X'xY'xZ. The ATI is used to assess the radio-responsiveness i.e. sensitivity or resistance to radiation treatment, of a cancer i.e. a tumour/neoplasm. In a preferred embodiment, the present invention relates to a method of generating the ATI, assessing the radio-responsiveness of a tumour/neoplasm based on the ATI and, based on the assessment, either treating or not treating the tumour with radiation. The present invention also relates to a method of determining if a cancer is likely to reoccur post radiation treatment comprising quantifying the level of manganese in voxels of a 3D region of a test sample from the cancer and determining the frequency of high metallomic regions (HMRs) in the cancer, wherein a high frequency of HMRs is indicative that the cancer is likely to reoccur and a low frequency of HMRs is indicative that the cancer is unlikely to reoccur; and associated methods of treatment. The invention further relates to a method of determining the radio-responsiveness of a melanoma, the method comprising determining the level of melanin in a test sample from the melanoma, wherein the lower the level of melanin the more sensitive the melanoma is to radiation and the higher the level of melanin the more resistant the melanoma is to radiation; and associated methods of treatment.

title: ATOMIC THERAPEUTIC INDICATOR
abstract: The invention relates to a method of determining the radio-responsiveness of a melanoma, the method comprising determining the level of melanin in a test sample from the melanoma, wherein the lower the level of melanin the more sensitive the melanoma is to radiation and the higher the level of melanin the more resistant the melanoma is to radiation; and associated methods of treatment.
claims: CLAIMS: 1. A method of generating an Atomic Therapeutic Indicator (ATI) of a biological test sample comprising quantifying the level of manganese in voxels in a 3D region of the test sample, wherein the method comprises: (a) selecting a 2D region of said test sample, wherein the 2D region is topographically defined by an X’:Y’ coordinate system wherein X’ is the length of the 2D region and Y’ is the breadth of the 2D region, wherein the 3D region corresponds to said 2D region and has a selected height represented by Z, wherein the 3D region is divided into voxels of a pre-defined volume, the volume of each voxel being defined by XxYxZ wherein X is the length of the voxel, Y is the breadth of the voxel and Z is the height of the voxel; (b) quantifying the level of manganese in each voxel; and (c) calculating the central tendency level of manganese in selected voxels; wherein the central tendency level of manganese in the selected voxels defines the ATI. 2. A method of determining the radio-responsiveness of a cancer, the method comprising generating an ATI of a test sample according to claim 1, wherein the lower the ATI the more sensitive the cancer is to radiation and the higher the ATI the more resistant the cancer is to radiation. 3. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein quantification of the level of manganese in the voxels of the test sample is calibrated using a reference standard, wherein the reference standard comprises one or more reference voxels, and wherein each reference voxel comprises a known quantity of manganese. 4. The method according to claim 3, wherein reference standard is a biological sample comprising a known quantity of endogenous or exogenous manganese. 5. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 4 wherein the ATI is expressed in calibrated counts per second (CC/S) 100 2016244193 11 Oct 2016 6. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the central tendency level is the median, arithmetic mean or mode. 7. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the level of manganese quantified in a 3D region of one or more control sample(s) is quantified concurrently, or sequentially in any order, when the test sample is quantified, or side-by-side with the test sample. 8. A method according to any one of claims 2 to 7, wherein the ATI is compared to a predetermined ATI threshold wherein the radio-responsiveness of the cancer is determined by assessing whether the ATI is above or below the ATI threshold, and wherein if the ATI is below the ATI threshold the cancer is determined to be sensitive to radiation; and wherein if the ATI is above the ATI threshold the cancer is determined to be resistant to radiation. 9. A method according to any one of claims 2 to 7, wherein the ATI is compared to two predetermined ATI thresholds wherein the radio-responsiveness of the cancer is determined by assessing whether the ATI is above or below the two thresholds, and wherein if the ATI is below the lower ATI threshold the cancer is determined to be sensitive to radiation; wherein if the ATI is above the second ATI threshold the cancer is determined to be resistant to radiation; and wherein if the ATI is between the two ATI thresholds the cancer is determined to be partially sensitive to radiation. 10. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 9 wherein the selected voxels are voxels in which cancer cells are detected. 101 2016244193 11 Oct 2016 11. A method according to claim 10, wherein the cancer cells are detected by visual inspection of the 2D region of the test sample stained with a stain that distinguishes cancer cells from other cells, and preferably the stain is hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) stain. 12. A method according to claim 10, wherein the cancer cells are detected by binding of an antibody, preferably a metal-labelled antibody, to the cancer cells. 13. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein X is in any range measurable and preferably in the range of about 1 micron to about 200 microns. 14. The method according to claim 13, wherein X is selected from about 10 to about 50 microns and any value in between, and preferably X is about 35 microns. 15. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein Y is in any range measurable and preferably in the range of about 1 micron to about 200 microns. 16. The method according to claim 15, wherein Y is selected from about 10 to about 50 microns and any value in between, and preferably Y is about 35 microns. 17. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein Z is in any range measurable and preferably in the range of about 1 micron to about 20 microns. 18. The method according to claim 17, wherein Z is selected from about 1 to about 20 microns and any value in between, preferably Z is about 1, or about 2, or about 3, or about 4, or about 5 microns. 19. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 12, X, Y and Z are selected respectively in ranges from about 1 to 200 microns, 1 to 200 microns, and 1 to 20 microns and any value in between in each range. 20. The method according to claim 19, wherein X, Y and Z are 35 microns, 35 microns and 5 microns respectively. 102 2016244193 11 Oct 2016 21. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the pre-defined volume of each voxel is in the range of about 1 cubic micron to about 8 x 10 5 cubic microns, or about 1 cubic micron to about 10,000 cubic microns, or about 2000 cubic microns to about 8,000 cubic microns. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the pre-defined volume is about 6,125 cubic microns. 23. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 22, wherein quantification of the level of manganese is in the voxels is by laser ablation-lnductively coupled plasma-mass spectrometry (LA-ICP-MS), laser ablation-time-of-flight-mass spectrometry (LA-TOF-MS), inductively coupled plasma-optical emission spectroscopy (ICP-OES), microwave plasmaatomic emission spectroscopy (MP-AES), laser induced break down spectroscopy (LIBS), secondary ion mass spectrometry (SIMS), X-ray absorption near edge structure (XANES), atomic absorption spectroscopy (AA) or X-ray fluorescence (XRF). 24. A method according to any one of claims 1 to 23, wherein the voxels are arranged in a track across the test sample, and quantifying the level of manganese is carried out by laser ablation of the track. 25. The method of claim any one of claims 1 to 24, wherein the test sample is selected from a cell, a population of cells, one or more single celled organism(s), a tissue sample, or part thereof, an organ sample or part thereof, one or more cells obtained/derived from a prokaryotic or eukaryotic organism, a population of cells and its associated non-cellular stromal components, a neoplastic cell or population of neoplastic cells, a tissue sample from any organ or tissue from a subject, a tumour, a solid mass or a “liquid” population of cells, cells of any cancer of the hematopoietic system including leukemic cells, the circulating cellular derivatives of solid tumours, and a cell or population of cells that has metastasized. 26. A method of treating cancer in a subject comprising performing the method of any one of claims 1 to 25 on a test sample from the subject, and including radiotherapy in treating said cancer in the subject if the test sample is determined to be sensitive to radiation. 27. A method of treating cancer in a subject comprising, performing the method of any one of claims 1 to 25 on a test sample of the subject, and not including radiotherapy in treating said cancer in the subject if the test sample is determined to be resistant to radiation. 28. The method according to claim 27, wherein the subject is a human. 103 2016244193 11 Oct 2016 29. The method according to any one of claims 7 to 28, wherein the control sample comprises or is derived from a cell, a population of cells, one or more single celled organism(s), a tissue sample, or part thereof, an organ sample or part thereof, one or more cells obtained/derived from a prokaryotic or eukaryotic organism, a population of cells and its associated non-cellular stromal components, a neoplastic cell or population of neoplastic cells, a tissue sample from any organ or tissue from a subject, a tumour wherein the tumour for example, is a solid mass or a “liquid” population of cells, any cancer of the hematopoietic system including leukemic cells, the circulating cellular derivatives of solid tumours, or a cell or population of cells that has metastasized. 30. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 29, wherein the test and/or the control sample comprises or is derived from a cell, a population of cells or a tissue sample of a tumour/neoplasm of breast cancer, prostate cancer, cancer of the testes, lymphoma, small cell lung cancer, cancer of the brain, mesothelioma or melanoma. 31. A method of determining the likelihood of reoccurrence of a cancer post radiation treatment comprising: (a) quantifying the level of manganese in a 3D region of a test sample from the cancer by selecting a 2D region of said test sample, wherein the 2D region is topographically defined by an X’:Y’ coordinate system wherein X’ is the length of the 2D region and Y’ is the breadth of the 2D region, wherein the 3D region corresponds to said 2D region and has a selected height represented by Z, wherein the 3D region is divided into three or more voxels of a pre-defined volume, the volume of each voxel being defined by XxYxZ, wherein X is the length of the voxel, Y is the breadth of the voxel and Z is the height of the voxel, and quantifying the level of manganese in each voxel; (b) identifying in the 2D region corresponding to the X and Y coordinates of the voxel, high metallomic regions (HMRs), being regions of the cancer in which the level of manganese is higher than in the surrounding areas as enabled by statistical thresholds that are multiples of a central tendency or any approximation between integers; wherein the higher the frequency of HMRs the higher the likelihood of the cancer reoccurring and the lower the frequency of HMRs the lower the likelihood of the cancer reoccurring. 104 2016244193 11 Oct 2016 32. A method according to claim 31, wherein the HMRs are also identified in the 2D region of the test sample stained with a stain that distinguishes cancer cells from stromal components, and preferably wherein the stain is hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) stain. 33. A method of determining the radio-responsiveness of a melanoma, the method comprising determining the level of melanin in a test sample from the melanoma, wherein the lower the level of melanin in the test sample the more sensitive the melanoma is to radiation and the higher the level of melanin in the test sample the more resistant the melanoma is to radiation. 34. A method of determining the radio-responsiveness of a melanoma, the method comprising comparing the level of melanin in a test sample from the melanoma to a predetermined melanin threshold wherein the radio-responsiveness of the melanoma is determined by assessing whether the level of melanin in the test sample is above or below the melanin threshold, wherein if the level of melanin in the test sample is below the melanin threshold the melanoma is determined to be sensitive to radiation; and wherein if the level of melanin in the test sample is above the melanin threshold the melanoma is determined to be resistant to radiation. 35. A method according to claim 33 or claim 34, wherein the level of melanin in the test sample is compared to two pre-determined melanin thresholds wherein the radio-responsiveness of the melanoma is determined by assessing whether the level of melanin in the test sample is above or below the two thresholds, and wherein if the melanin in the test sample is below the lower melanin threshold the melanoma is determined to be sensitive to radiation; wherein if the melanin in the test sample is above the higher melanin threshold the melanoma is determined to be resistant to radiation; and 105 2016244193 11 Oct 2016 wherein if the melanin in the test sample is between the two melanin thresholds, the melanoma is determined to be partially sensitive to radiation. 36. A method of treating melanoma in a subject comprising performing the method of any one of claims 33 to 35 on a test sample from the subject, and including radiotherapy in treating said melanoma in the subject if the melanoma is determined to be sensitive to radiation. 37. A method of treating melanoma in a subject comprising, performing the method of any one of claims 33 to 35 on a test sample from the subject, and not including radiotherapy in treating said melanoma in the subject if the melanoma is determined to be resistant to radiation. 2016244193 11 Oct 2016 Figure 1 iVS&SSSi' C's ... title: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR ABNORMALITY DETECTION
abstract: #$%^&*AU2017279806B220231012.pdf##### H:14dxlaer vekNRPobl\DCC\DXL\1623!047 Ldox7- 22 ! 2 / 2 017 ABSTRACT An abnormality determination system for determining an abnormality of an individual within a learning environment, including: a monitor, including one or more imaging devices, said imaging devices configured to generate imaging signals; and an abnormality detection server device, including: a communications interface to receive data; at least one computer processor to execute program instructions; and a memory, coupled to the at least one computer processor, to store program instructions for execution by the at least one computer processor to automatically: receive imaging signals from the monitor; process the imaging signals to generate imaging data; process the imaging data to detect the individual, and to generate corresponding identity data representing the identity of the individual; and identify an abnormality of the individual based on at least one of: physical detection data representing the detection of a physical characteristic of the individual from the imaging data, and a corresponding physical profile of the individual; and behavioural detection data representing the detection of a behavioural characteristic of the individual from the imaging data, and a corresponding behavioural profile of the individual, and context data representing a context of the detected behavioural or physical characteristics within the learning environment. 8/10 800 Receive imaging data 802 Processimagingdatatodetectindividual 804 Generate an indication of identity for the individual 805 Generate physical detection data Generate behavioural detection for the individual 806 data for the individual 808 Identifyanabnormalityfortheindividual 810 Report the abnormality 812 Update biometricprofile 814 Fig. 8
claims: 2017279806 04 Sep 2023 -62THE CLAIMS DEFINING THE INVENTION ARE AS FOLLOWS: 1. An abnormality determination system for determining an abnormality of an individual within a learning environment, including: a monitor, including one or more imaging devices, said imaging devices configured to generate imaging signals; and at least one abnormality detection server device, including: a communications interface to receive data; at least one computer processor to execute program instructions; and a memory, coupled to the at least one computer processor, storing program instructions for execution by the at least one computer processor to automatically: receive imaging signals from the monitor; process the imaging signals to generate imaging data; process the imaging data to detect the individual, and to generate corresponding identity data representing the identity of the individual; and identify an abnormality of the individual based on: physical detection data representing the detection of a physical characteristic of the individual from the imaging data, and a corresponding physical profile of the individual; and behavioural detection data representing the detection of a behavioural characteristic of the individual from the imaging data, and a corresponding behavioural profile of the individual, and context data representing a context of the detected behavioural or physical characteristic within the learning environment, wherein the context includes one or more influencing factors that influence the physical characteristic or behavioural characteristic of the individual; wherein identifying the abnormality of the individual includes: adjusting the corresponding physical profile or behavioural profile of the individual based on the context data. 2017279806 04 Sep 2023 2. The abnormality determination system of claim 1, wherein the imaging signals include: video signals; and thermal imaging signals, including a series of thermal images that are associated with corresponding images of the video signals, and where the video signals and thermal imaging signals are generated by a first imaging device and a second imaging device respectively, said video signals and thermal imaging signals being processed by the abnormality detection server device to generate one or more video and thermal image frames including the individual. 3. The abnormality determination system of any one of claims 1 to 2, wherein determining a behavioural characteristic of the individual includes: processing the behavioural detection data to determine that the behavioural characteristic is one of a behaviour type including: a gesture; and a facial expression; comparing the behavioural detection data to one or more behavioural characteristic models of the determined behaviour type; and selecting a particular behavioural characteristic based on a result of the comparison, where the behavioural characteristics are specific to the learning environment and include: for the gesture behaviour type: (i) Head-scratching; (ii) Hitting/striking; (iii) Nodding; (iv) Hand-raising; (v) Waving; and (vi) Thumbs up/down; and for the facial expression behaviour type: (i) Smiling; (ii) Frowning; and (iii) Crying. 4. The abnormality determination system of claim 3, wherein the behavioural characteristics include: for the gesture behaviour type: (vii) one or more user-defined gestures, said user-defined gestures being gestures that are customised and/or configured specifically for the abnormality determination system. 5. The abnormality determination system of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the abnormality detection server device is configured to selectively update the physical profile and the behavioural profile of the individual based on corresponding physical detection data and behavioural detection data. 2017279806 04 Sep 2023 6. The abnormality determination system of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the abnormality detection server device is configured to: transmit, to a management system device of the learning environment, an indication of the identity of the individual; transmit, to the management system device, an indication of an abnormality identified for the individual; and receive, from the management system device, context data representing a context for detected behavioural or physical characteristics of the individual. 7. The abnormality determination system of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the context data indicates one or more of: the types of learning activities being undertaken by the individual; and the highest degree of physical activity which the individual has engaged in within a particular time period. 8. title: ASSEMBLY LINE WITH INTEGRATED ELECTRONIC VISUAL INSPECTION
abstract: Methods and systems are disclosed for obtaining a first image of a tray, determining a presence or absence of one or more first patterns in the first image, determining a rotation of each the one or more first patterns in the first image, and performing an action based on the presence or absence and the rotation of the one or more first patterns in the first image.
claims: 2017301504 21 May 2021 The claims defining the invention are as follows: 1. A system comprising: a belt; a plurality of imagers, each configured to: obtain an image of its respective field of view, analyze the image to determine a quantity and/or orientation of one or more items within its respective field of view, and generate a pass inspection signal or a fail inspection signal, based on the quantity and/or orientation of the one or more items within its respective field of view; wherein at least one of the plurality of imagers is further configured to count a number of a plurality of trays disposed on the belt, wherein a number of empty tray locations are interspersed between the plurality of trays; and a processor, coupled to each of the plurality of imagers, configured to, receive the pass inspection signal or the fail inspection signal, and advance the belt based on receiving a pass inspection signal from each of the plurality of imagers. 2.
