CLAIMS 1. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) System characterized by comprising a robotic platform, an automatic loading system, an intelligent integrated management subsystem, an on-board intelligence module and Command and Control Stations. 2. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) system, according to claim 1, characterized in that it can be used with different types of batteries on different robotic platforms. 3. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) System, according to claim 1, characterized by the fact that the robotic platform comprises a geopositioning system, cameras, optronic sensors (such as RGB, thermal, Corona), anti-collision system, laser scanning system, on-board computer and digital data communication links. 4. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) System, according to claim 1, characterized by the fact that the robotic platform is interchangeable with various applications and performs operational reconfiguration according to demand. 5. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) system according to claim 1, characterized in that the automatic charging subsystem comprises an intelligent and adaptive microprocessor circuit. 6. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) System, according to claim 5, characterized by the fact that the intelligent microprocessor circuit automatically configures a self-charger to charge the batteries of the aerial platform and adjacent systems. 7. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) System, according to claim 1, characterized by the fact that the modular ERP-type architecture contains the intelligent integrated management subsystem as the main module. 8. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) System, according to claim 1, characterized by the fact that the intelligent integrated management subsystem comprises a multi-user and multi-system Interface Module. 9. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) System, according to claim 9, characterized by the fact that the Interface Module connects the SIGI to the Command and Control Centers, the Control Stations and the sensors (IoT). 10. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) System, according to claim 1, characterized by the fact that the embedded intelligence module is developed through the use of Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision, for the recognition and identification of assets, in a parameterized and regionalized manner. 11. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) System, according to claim 1, characterized by the fact that the Command and Control Stations comprise computing devices mounted on a mobile platform. 12. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) System, according to claim 1, characterized by the fact that it acts with analysis algorithms and automatic identification of defects, inconsistencies, diagnosis and application of artificial intelligence for asset registration, using 3D mapping to map vegetation growth. 13. Method of identifying and registering assets, monitoring and inspecting energy transmission and distribution lines, characterized by the fact that it comprises the steps of remote automatic identification and differentiation of assets and use of the information collected to generate reports of real-time situational analysis and prospecting of asset losses as well as reports for their maintenance. 14. Method of identifying and registering assets, monitoring and inspecting energy transmission and distribution lines, according to claim 14, characterized by the fact of performing post-processing of data in the integrated management system (SIGI). 15. Method of identifying and registering assets, monitoring and inspecting energy transmission and distribution lines, according to claim 14, characterized by the fact that it acts with analysis algorithms and automatic identification of defects, inconsistencies, diagnosis and application of artificial intelligence for asset registration, using 3D mapping to map vegetation growth. DESCRIPTIVE REPORT Application for an Invention Patent for “UNMANNED AERIAL VEHICLE SYSTEM, METHOD FOR IDENTIFYING AND REGISTERING ASSETS, MONITORING AND INSPECTING ENERGY TRANSMISSION AND DISTRIBUTION LINES” FIELD OF INVENTION [001] The invention is multidisciplinary in nature, involving the fields of Aeronautical, Computer, Electrical and Electronic Engineering, in the development of tools for the inspection of power transmission and distribution lines, more specifically an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle System (UAV) for registering assets, their maintenance and network inspection, featuring an automatic recharging system and an Intelligent Integrated Management System. STATE OF THE TECHNIQUE [002] Currently, in the high and low voltage distribution area, images are collected for analysis by the operator, so that, at a later stage, defects can be analyzed via software. Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are mainly used to collect images over long distances. [003] In addition, there are also projects to carry out visual inspections on distribution lines. However, in these studies, the robots are fixed to the line and/or do not allow for the collection of 3D images, or even with a geopositioning system being processed in real time, which in a certain way increases the accuracy of the images and the quality of the information obtained. [004] The self-loading UAV developed performs flights close to distribution and transmission networks, collecting georeferenced images in the visible and/or multispectral spectrum, such as thermal images with radiometry. The collection of images allows the 3D mapping of the asset, analyzing it, identifying it, registering it in the sequence and, at the same time, identifying possible defects. [005] 3D analysis, automatic registration of assets in real time, mapping of vegetation growth, with an Intelligent Integrated Management System, is an innovative approach not yet carried out in R&D projects in Brazil. [006] In summary, the system and method developed work with analysis algorithms and automatic identification of defects, inconsistencies, diagnosis and application of artificial intelligence for asset registration using 3D mapping, vegetation growth mapping and self-charging of the UAV using distribution and transmission lines. [007] Patent application PI 0904898-7, of the same ownership as the present application, discloses a Multipurpose Aerial Monitoring Platform that allows the controller to remotely control an aircraft or other robotic devices through a command and control station. [008] The multipurpose aerial monitoring platform has several applications in the military and civil areas, such as aerial monitoring of high voltage transmission lines, roads, gas pipelines, urban areas, topographic surveys, various mapping, such as environmental and risk areas, and also as a development platform for robotic, aeronautical and similar systems where a mobile and versatile aerial platform is required, which allows or does not allow its integration with other technological systems. [009] However, it is worth noting that the aforementioned state of the art document does not reveal or suggest a self-charging system, with 3D mapping, vegetation growth mapping and corona effects analysis. [010] Document BR 10 2015 032214 3 refers to an image generation system, with global georeferenced registration, obtained from a small UAV. The use of such a system allows the use of small aircraft to map and monitor areas of the Earth's surface. [011] The invention of document BR 10 2015 032214 3 comprises the development and construction of a prototype of an aircraft specially designed to contain the components related to the mapping and monitoring application, as well as the image processing system dedicated to the global georeferenced recording of the images obtained from the aircraft, without the use of a stabilization and balance device and with the storage of the generated images and post-processing at the control station, using the image mosaic reconstruction technique and subsequent conversion into a georeferenced mosaic. [012] Document BR 10 2015 032214 3 does not reveal a self-loading UAV (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle) System that performs automatic remote registration of assets and uses the information collected to generate asset loss reports as well as reports for their maintenance. [013] Document CN114604110A discloses an unmanned aerial vehicle wireless charging control system and method; and the system comprises an unmanned aerial vehicle comprising an unmanned aerial vehicle coil; the charging platform comprises a parking apron, a plurality of power transmission coils, and a plurality of induction detection coils. [014] An induction detection coil coincides with a power transmission coil, and each induction detection coil is connected to a voltage detector; After the unmanned aerial vehicle lands on the parking lot, the coil of the unmanned aerial vehicle is coupled to all the induction detection coils, the energy is wirelessly transmitted to all the induction detection coils, and then the position of the unmanned aerial vehicle is determined according to the voltage value of each induction detection coil. [015] The power transmission coil corresponding to the induction detection coil with the maximum voltage detection value wirelessly transmits power to the coil of the unmanned aerial vehicle so as to charge the unmanned aerial vehicle; the position of the unmanned aerial vehicle does not need to be moved by adopting a centering mechanism. [016] Wireless power transmission is realized for the unmanned aerial vehicle by discovering a power transmission coil which is arranged in a parking lot and has the largest coupling degree with a coil of unmanned aerial vehicle, and the charging efficiency is improved; when the induction detection coil works. Only a small current is needed, and electric energy is saved. [017] Document CN114604110A also does not disclose a self-loading UAV (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle) System that performs automatic remote asset registration and uses the information collected to generate asset loss reports as well as reports for asset maintenance. [018] The self-loading UAV (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle) System developed has two main purposes: monitoring for the survey and automated registration of assets, such as transformers, insulators, cables, poles, public lighting and other assets and their respective inspections in distribution and transmission lines. [019] Additionally, through an Intelligent Integrated Management System (SIGI), the UAV will use the information collected to generate reports of the real situational analysis found by the robotic platform during mapping. Such reports may reference asset losses as well as reports for their maintenance. For the UAV to be able to fulfill the two listed purposes, a self-charging system will enable an increase in efficiency, allowing for increased autonomy in operation that will be greater in relation to the systems available on the market. [020] Thus, the proposed development reduces costs in the asset registration process. The innovations proposed in this patent application are directly related to the UAV self-loading system, the vegetative control of easement areas and the like, and the automatic asset registration. [021] Furthermore, for the asset registration to be carried out, Computer Vision and Artificial Intelligence algorithms (also related to Big Data) are applied to process, analyze and inform what the data is. [022] The proposed development considers low voltage power distribution systems and high voltage transmission lines. DESIGNS [023] Figure 1 shows the basic diagram of the Intelligent Integrated Management System (SIGI) software with the interfaces and relationships between the devices. [024] Figure 2 illustrates the schematic diagram of the developed system, where it is possible to observe the receiving and transmitting coils of the circuit. [025] Figure 3 shows the BRV-03 robotic platform, where the data connections, anti-collision system, front camera and GPS are indicated. [026] Figure 4 shows the Mobile Command and Control Station, developed on a vehicle platform, being fully customized for this operation. [027] Figure 5 shows the Portable Command Station, developed on a portable platform, being installed at the location of interest to provide local infrastructure for the operation. DETAILED DESCRIPTION OF THE INVENTION [028] The invention describes a Multipurpose Aerial Monitoring Platform (UAV), for registering assets and inspecting energy transmission and distribution lines, with an automatic recharging system and an Intelligent Integrated Management System. [029] The Automatic Power Line Inspection tool seeks to solve the problem of maintenance inspection and management of the structure of Power Lines and related assets, where the UAV and related tools, for example, SIGI management software, Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision procedures assist predictive maintenance procedures, increasing the robustness of the electrical network. [030] The development of the tool aims to solve the problem of inspection maintenance and management of the structure of voltage line assets, automatically, using robotic platforms to fly over selected areas and perform data acquisition and processing. [031] Furthermore, it is worth noting that the systems are monitored by dedicated software running within the Command and Control Station (CCS), to increase the effectiveness of this technology. [032] The entire process is monitored and controlled by customizable software called the Intelligent Integrated Management System (SIGI), an integrated enterprise management system (ERP). The SIGI software can manage data, connect sensors (IoT) and analyze results according to authority requests. [033] Based on the data collected from the areas of interest, the SIGI software shows in real time the situational analysis of these areas and allows the administrator to optimize resources (material and human) improving the efficiency of resource allocation in these areas. [034] In addition to the development of management software, the invention relates to the development of sensors to collect information in the field and update this information to the management software database. [035] Furthermore, the invention relates to the development of a wireless robotic platform charging station, such as a drone, navigation and control methodology using Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision based on COTS (Commercial-Of-The-Shelf) equipment and management software. [036] The developed system has the addition of technologies that refine the self-charging process and make it intelligent, making it possible to use different types of batteries on different robotic platforms. [037] Such technologies involve an intelligent and adaptive microprocessor circuit, which identifies the type of robotic platform, automatically configures the autocharger, and internet connectivity, enabling the user to remotely control and monitor the recharging of the robotic platform. [038] The robotic platform developed has a modular architecture, based on COTS (Commercial-of-the-Shelf) equipment, that is, off-the-shelf equipment, where it is possible to include Corona sensors, without the systemic decharacterization of the equipment. [039] In addition, the system also has embedded intelligence, through the use of Artificial Intelligence, for the recognition and identification of assets, in a parameterized and regionalized manner. In a complementary manner, the methods used post-process the data to obtain greater assertiveness and improve the intended results. [040] The developed multipurpose monitoring platform is an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) system. In this sense, it is worth highlighting that the Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) system comprises, as main elements, a robotic platform, an automatic charging system, an intelligent integrated management subsystem, an on-board intelligence module and a Command and Control Station. [041] The robotic platform has a modular design and uses a COTS (Commercial-off-the-Shelf) concept for simple maintenance and component replacement. The structure is made of composite materials, which makes it strong and lightweight. The robotic platforms are capable of performing small internal data processing, such as obtaining images, georeferencing and identifying objects, scanning/mapping areas, redirecting mapping, and other functions involving machine learning. In other words, the robotic platform is interchangeable with various applications and performs operational redirection according to demand. [042] The robotic platform comprises a geopositioning system, which may be of the GPS type - RTK/PPK, 4x RGB Camera, optronic sensors (such as RGB, thermal, Corona), Thermal Camera, NIR camera, Anti-collision system, laser scanning system, Onboard Computer (Intelligent robotic platform), 4G LTE Communication (BVLOS), where the sensors can be customized according to the application and new market demands. [043] The automatic charging system is carried out by transmitting electrical energy from one point to another through a vacuum or atmosphere without the use of electrical wires or any other contact material. [044] Wireless power transmission (WPT) is the process that occurs in any system where electromagnetic (EM) energy is transmitted from a power source to an electrical load, without artificial interconnecting conductors. [045] Robotic platforms can be equipped with various devices that use electricity, such as sensors, high-resolution and infrared cameras, tracking and the global positioning system. They can be used as equipment for monitoring agricultural fields, railway lines and power transmission lines. [046] In the developed system, the battery can be charged at the base station of the robotic platform, through a self-inductance system. In addition, it is carried out with a circuit model using an induction coil with a low-frequency core to power batteries through wireless charging with the aim of supplying energy to the battery of a robotic platform. [047] The intelligent integrated management subsystem (SIGI) is responsible for the entire process of monitoring the inspection and management of the power line asset structure. SIGI can manage data, connect sensors (loT) and analyze the results according to the authority's requests. Furthermore, it is worth noting that the intelligent integrated management subsystem comprises a multi-user and multi-system Interface Module. [048] Based on the data collected from the areas of interest, SIGI shows in real time the situational analysis of these areas and allows the administrator to optimize resources (material and human) improving the efficiency of resource allocation in these areas. In addition to the development of management software, the development of sensors to collect information in the field and update this information to the management software database is planned. [049] As seen in Figure 1, the basic SIGI diagram relates to the interfaces and devices, in addition to comprising: [050] SIGI main module: This software is designed as an ERP software, with a modular concept that allows customization of functionalities according to the application. The main block of this module contains the main ERP software, with web access, database, connectivity with Drone software and APP software. [051] Interface Module: Allows SIGI connectivity with field Command and Control Centers (C2), such as the Camera Monitoring Room, ground team connectivity, and communicates with any type of infrastructure, whether local or remote, with internet access. [052] Field Module: Represents the devices that will be used in the field, with the collection of information generated by sensors (loT), robotic platforms, customized according to the mission profile. [053] Operational results: The diagram shown in Figure 1 represents the results of the application, in terms of reports, predictive models and big data, to assist managers in decision making. In this case, the operational results will be obtained according to systemic customizations, according to the definition of the object to be managed. [054] Robotic platform: In this example, of the Quadcopter type (which can be varied according to the operational demand, from aerial, surface and terrestrial platforms), shown in Figure 3, it acquires images and executes artificial intelligence procedures of the inspected areas. [055] In addition, the UAV System comprises the onboard intelligence module, which is developed through the use of Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision, for the recognition and identification of assets, in a parameterized and regionalized manner. [056] Furthermore, the Command and Control Stations stand out, such as the Mobile Command and Control Vehicle, in Figure 4, and the DroneBox, in Figure 5. The Mobile Command and Control Vehicle involves the development of computing devices mounted on a mobile platform, capable of constantly monitoring the operation of the robotic platform, collecting data in the field and interfacing with the Integrated Command and Control Center, through data links, such as the internet. The same feature applies to DroneBox, but as a main feature, DroneBox is used in a fixed and punctual way. [057] The Command and Control Station (CCS) monitors the Unmanned Aerial System in real time, from takeoff to landing. [058] Furthermore, the proposed invention also defines a method for remote automatic registration of assets, such as transformers, insulators, cables and other assets and inspections on distribution lines, posts (mutual use) and public lighting. Furthermore, the method allows, through an Intelligent Integrated Management System (SIGI), the use of the information collected to generate reports on asset losses as well as reports for the maintenance of these assets. [059] Thus, it is clarified that the developed method performs data acquisition in the field, the embedded processing (Embedded Intelligence), in a complementary manner, performs post-processing of the data in the integrated management system (SIGI), where there is an interface with the data from the Distributor's Geographic Database (BDGD) and, in a comparative manner, performs data analysis, generating specific maintenance reports, asset losses and illustrates the efficiency of the tool, through statistical data.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR GENERATING DIGITAL INFORMATION AND ALTERING DIGITAL MODELS OF COMPONENTS WITH SAME; SYSTÈME ET PROCÉDÉ PERMETTANT DE GÉNÉRER DES INFORMATIONS NUMÉRIQUES ET DE MODIFIER DES MODÈLES NUMÉRIQUES DE COMPOSANTS AVEC CES DERNIÈRES
abstract:
Digital information to be associated with a component can be generated and used to alter a digital model of the component. A line of sight to at least a portion of a marking device and the component can be detected, and at least one of an orientation or a position of an endpoint of the marking device relative to the component can be determined using the line of sight. Digital information indicative of the at least one of the orientation or the position of the endpoint relative to the component can be generated responsive to the endpoint of the marking device contacting the component. The digital information can be used to alter a digital model based on defects of the component corresponding to portions contacted by the endpoint of the marking device.

La présente invention concerne des informations numériques à associer à un composant qui peuvent être générées et utilisés pour modifier un modèle numérique du composant. Une ligne de visée vers au moins une partie d'un dispositif de marquage et le composant peut être détectée et une orientation et/ou une position d'un point d'extrémité du dispositif de marquage par rapport au composant peuvent être déterminées à l'aide de la ligne de visée. Des informations numériques indiquant l'orientation et/ou la position du point d'extrémité par rapport au composant peuvent être générées à la suite de la mise en contact du point d'extrémité du dispositif de marquage avec le composant. Les informations numériques peuvent être utilisées pour modifier un modèle numérique en se basant sur des défauts du composant correspondant à des parties mises en contact par le point d'extrémité du dispositif de marquage.
claims: What is claimed is: 1. A system for generating digital information associated with a component and altering a digital model of the component using the digital information, the system comprising: a marking device including an endpoint disposed at an end of an elongate body; and a computing device including a memory, a processor, and an imaging device, wherein the memory includes instructions executable by the processor to: determine at least one of an orientation or a position of the endpoint relative to the component detected using a line of sight of the imaging device; generate the digital information responsive to the endpoint of the marking device contacting the component, wherein the digital information is indicative of the at least one of the orientation or the position; and alter the digital model according to the digital information. 2. The system of claim 1 , wherein a first target is coupled to the portion of the marking device and a second target is coupled to the component. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein at least one of the first target or the second target includes a retroreflective fabric detectable by the imaging device. 4. The system of claim 2, wherein the instructions to determine the least one of the orientation or the position of the endpoint relative to the component using the line of sight of the imaging device include instructions executable by the processor to: calibrate at least one of an orientation or a position of the first target relative to the second target; and calculate the least one of the orientation or the position of the endpoint relative to the component based on offsets between orientations or positions of the first target to the endpoint and the second target to a portion of the component contacted by the endpoint. 5. The system of claim 2, wherein the instructions to generate the digital information responsive to the endpoint of the marking device contacting the component include instructions executable by the processor to: identify a contact between the endpoint of the marking device and a portion of the component; and receive a command to associate the portion of the component with a defect of a list of defects detectable for the component, wherein the digital information includes data associating the defect and the portion of the component. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the instructions to alter the digital model according to the digital information include instructions executable by the processor to: receive a first digital file including the digital model; and process instructions to alter a portion of the digital model corresponding to the portion of the component based on the digital information, wherein an altered digital model generated responsive to the alter is stored in one of the first digital file or a second digital file including the digital information. 7. The system of claim 5, wherein the instructions to identify the contact between the endpoint of the marking device and the portion of the component include instructions executable by the processor to: determine a set of coordinates representing points of contact between the endpoint of the marking device and portions of the component in a multi-dimensional space, wherein the set of coordinates forms a point cloud; project a surface of the point cloud to a surface of the component corresponding to the set of coordinates; and identify the portion of the component associated with the contact as at least a portion of the surface of the point cloud. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the endpoint of the marker device receives an adapter to facilitate contact between the endpoint and the component. 9. A method for generating digital information associated with a component and altering a digital model of the component using the digital information, the method comprising: detecting a line of sight to at least a portion of a marking device and the component; determining at least one of an orientation or a position of an endpoint of the marking device relative to the component using the line of sight; generating the digital information responsive to the endpoint of the marking device contacting the component, wherein the digital information is indicative of the at least one of the orientation or the position of the endpoint relative to the component; and altering the digital model according to the digital information. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein a first target is coupled to the portion of the marking device and a second target is coupled to the component. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein at least one of the first target or the second target includes a retroreflective fabric detectable by the imaging device. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein determining at least one of the orientation or the position of the endpoint of the marking device relative to the component using the line of sight comprises: calibrating at least one of an orientation or a position of the first target relative to the second target; and calculating the least one of the orientation or the position of the endpoint relative to the component based on offsets between orientations or positions of the first target to the endpoint and the second target to a portion of the component contacted by the endpoint. 13. The method of claim 10, wherein generating the digital information responsive to the endpoint of the marking device contacting the component comprises: identifying a contact between the endpoint of the marking device and a portion of the component; and receiving a command to associate the portion of the component with a defect of a list of defects detectable for the component, wherein the digital information includes data associating the defect and the portion of the component. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein altering the digital model according to the digital information comprises: receiving a first digital file including the digital model; and processing instructions to alter a portion of the digital model corresponding to the portion of the component based on the digital information, wherein an altered digital model generated responsive to the altering is stored in one of the first digital file or a second digital file including the digital information. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein identifying the contact between the endpoint of the marking device and the portion of the component comprises: determining a set of coordinates representing points of contact between the endpoint of the marking device and portions of the component in a multi-dimensional space, wherein the set of coordinates forms a point cloud; projecting a surface of the point cloud to a surface of the component corresponding to the set of coordinates; and identifying the portion of the component associated with the contact as at least a portion of the surface of the point cloud. 16. The method of claim 9, wherein the endpoint of the marker device receives an adapter to facilitate contact between the endpoint and the component. 17. A system for generating digital information associated with a component and altering a digital model of the component using the digital information, the system comprising: a marking device including an endpoint disposed at an end of an elongate body and a first target coupled to the elongate body; an imaging device including a line of sight detecting the first target and a second target coupled to the component; and a computing device including a memory and a processor, wherein the memory includes instructions executable by the processor to: calibrate the first target to the second target based on positions and orientations of the marking device and the component detected in the line of sight; determine a contact between the endpoint and a portion of the component based on positions and orientations of the endpoint with respect to the portion of the component detected using the calibrated first and second targets; receive a command to associate the portion of the component with a defect of a list of defects detectable for the component; generate the digital information to indicate an association between the portion of the component and the defect; and alter a portion of the digital model corresponding to the portion of the component based on the digital information. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the instructions to determine the contact between the endpoint and the portion of the component based on the positions and orientations of the endpoint with respect to the portion of the component detected using the calibrated first and second targets include instructions executable by the processor to: determine a set of coordinates representing points of contact between the endpoint of the marking device and portions of the component in a multi-dimensional space, wherein the set of coordinates forms a point cloud; project a surface of the point cloud to a surface of the component corresponding to the set of coordinates; and identify the portion of the component contacted by the endpoint as at least a portion of the surface of the point cloud. 19. The system of claim 17, wherein entries of the list of defects detectable for the component include associated metadata indicating a technique for altering portions of the digital model based on corresponding defects, and wherein portions of the associated metadata can be included in the command such that the digital information can be generated to include the portions of the associated metadata. 20. The system of claim 17, wherein the command is received from at least one of the marking device, a user interface of the computing device, or an input device in communication with at least one of the marking device or the computing device.

title: REMOTE MONITORING METHOD USING IMAGE PROCESSING INTELLIGENCE; PROCÉDÉ DE SURVEILLANCE À DISTANCE UTILISANT UNE INTELLIGENCE DE TRAITEMENT D'IMAGE
abstract:
A method of remote facilities monitoring for the detection of contamination, leaks or failures in petrochemical and related facilities. Remote cameras are used to capture images of equipment to be inspected, which are transmitted to a central server. A software component on the central server applies artificial intelligence and image processing methods to detect the presence of anomalies in the images for display to an operator and potential subsequent dispatch of in person follow-up. Parallel data streams from sensors at the remote sites can be used by the software on the server to enhance the level of confidence in anomaly detection. The server software uses an adaptive object-detection function to analyze image data and learns over time to provide enhanced detection of interest regions and failure conditions.

L'invention concerne un procédé de surveillance d'installations à distance pour la détection d'une contamination, de fuites ou de défaillances dans des installations pétrochimiques et associées. Des caméras à distance sont utilisées pour capturer des images d'un équipement à inspecter, qui sont transmises à un serveur central. Un composant logiciel sur le serveur central applique une intelligence artificielle et des procédés de traitement d'image pour détecter la présence d'anomalies dans les images pour affichage à un opérateur et une éventuelle répartition ultérieure de suivi en personne. Des flux de données parallèles provenant de capteurs au niveau des sites distants peuvent être utilisés par le logiciel sur le serveur pour améliorer le niveau de confiance dans la détection d'anomalie. Le logiciel serveur utilise une fonction de détection d'objet adaptative pour analyser des données d'image et apprend avec le temps à fournir une détection améliorée de régions d'intérêt et de conditions de défaillance.
claims: What is claimed is: 1. A method of remote visual inspection and failure detection at a plurality of monitoring locations, said method comprising: a. providing an endpoint device at each monitoring location, each endpoint device having: i. a capture bus capable of receiving image data from at least one connected digital camera; ii. at least one digital camera positioned with a camera view of the monitoring location, and connected to the capture bus; iii. a network interface by which the endpoint device can communicate with a remote server; and iv. endpoint client software capable of facilitating the capture of images from the at least one camera and the construction and transmission of image data transmissions to a remote server; b. providing a server comprising: i. a processor and memory; ii. a network interface via which the server can communicate with external devices including the endpoint devices; iii. at least one human interface device operatively connected thereto permitting graphic display and user interaction with a user; and iv. a monitoring software component comprising the necessary processor instructions to facilitate the conduct of the method via the server, said monitoring software component including an adaptive object-detection function; c. periodically executing an image capture step at each endpoint device by: i. capturing an image from at least one digital camera connected to the capture bus of the endpoint device; ii. processing the image data corresponding to each captured image for transmission to the server as an image data transmission; and iii. transmitting the image data transmission to the server via the network; d. conducting an extraction step on receipt of an image data transmission at the server by extracting an extracted image in respect of each captured camera view received from the endpoint device; e. contextualizing each monitoring location at least once in respect of each camera view by: i. selecting an extracted image of the camera view recovered from the conduct of an extraction step; ii. applying the object-detection function to detect the presence of any focus cues within the extracted image signifying interest regions corresponding to high-probability failure areas for ongoing visual inspection; iii. using each identified focus cue to calculate a desired dimension and location of each interest region in the extracted image; and iv. storing the dimensions and location of each interest region in respect of the corresponding camera view, now being a contextualized camera view; f. in a monitoring loop, using the server and monitoring software component to detect the presence of failure conditions requiring human inspection by, on completion of an extraction step in respect of a received image data transmission pertaining to a contextualized camera view: i. selecting the extracted image of the contextualized camera view; ii. retrieving the stored dimensions and location of each interest region in the contextualized camera view; iii. applying the object-detection function to the defined interest regions of the extracted image to detect the presence of any visible failure conditions therein; iv. on detection of any visible failure conditions within an interest region, executing a notification step by: 1. displaying to a user via a human interface device at least the portion of the extracted image corresponding to the interest region containing the detected failure condition; 2. permitting the user to validate, reject or modify the detected failure conditions; and 3. storing any user validation, rejection or modification interactions; and v. continuing the monitoring loop; wherein the monitoring software component will adapt the object-detection function based upon the stored user interactions to enhance its level of accuracy and certainty in future object detections. 2. The method of Claim 1 wherein the notification step further comprises displaying additional information to the user selected from the group of: a. visual identification of the failure attributes detected and contributing to the determination of the existence of the failure condition; or b. a calculated confidence score in respect of the identified failure condition. 3. The method of Claim 1 wherein an endpoint device has more than one camera connected thereto. 4. The method of Claim 3 wherein the camera view of more than one camera encompasses the same area of the monitoring location, whereby the level of certainty in failure detection within the monitoring location is maximized by processing of multiple camera view images. 5. The method of Claim 1 wherein the focus cues which will be recognized by the object- detection function are selected from the group of: a. detected interfaces between equipment and the environment; b. detected interfaces between components of equipment; c. elements of the surrounding environment; d. detected equipment which has a purpose related to leak containment, mitigation, fluid presence or tank levels; or e. physical framing elements positioned in the view of the corresponding cameras. 6. The method of Claim 1 wherein the contextualization step in respect of a particular monitoring location and camera view further comprises, following the dimensioning and location of the interest regions: a. using a human interface device, displaying the extracted image to a user via a human interface device along with an overlay of the position and dimension of each interest region, and permitting the user to validate, reject or modify the dimensions and location of the interest regions; and b. adapting the object-detection function based upon the user interactions validating, rejecting or modifying the detected focus cues and the dimensions and locations of the interest regions to enhance its level of accuracy and certainty in future contextualization and interest region rendering. 7. The method of Claim 1 wherein the contextualization step is conducted in respect of only the first image data transmission received from an endpoint device in respect of a camera view. 8. The method of Claim 1 wherein the contextualization step is conducted in respect of each camera view contained within each image data transmission received from an endpoint device. 9. title: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR TRAINING INSPECTION EQUIPMENT FOR AUTOMATIC DEFECT CLASSIFICATION; PROCÉDÉ ET SYSTÈME D'ENTRAÎNEMENT D'ÉQUIPEMENT D'INSPECTION POUR LA CLASSIFICATION AUTOMATIQUE DE DÉFAUTS
abstract:
A computer-implemented method, an inspection system and a non-transitory storage medium are provided, for automatically generating defect classification models, using machine learning, for the inspection of semiconductor and/or Printed Circuit Board (PCB) parts. The defect classification models are automatically built from a first combination of a trained neural network binary classifier and optimizer and from a second combination of a trained neural network multi-class classifier and optimizer.

L'invention concerne un procédé mis en œuvre par ordinateur, un système d'inspection et un support de stockage non transitoire, servant à générer automatiquement des modèles de classification de défauts, au moyen d'un apprentissage automatique, pour l'inspection de composants à semi-conducteur et/ou de parties d'une carte de circuit imprimé (PCB). Les modèles de classification de défauts sont construits automatiquement à partir d'une première combinaison d'un classifieur binaire à réseau de neurones entraîné et d'un optimiseur et à partir d'une seconde combinaison d'un classifieur multiclasse à réseau de neurones entraîné et d'un optimiseur.
claims: CLAIMS: 1. A computer-implemented method for automatically generating a defect classification model, using machine learning, for use in an automated inspection system for the inspection of semiconductor and/or Printed Circuit Board (PCB) parts, the method comprising the steps of: acquiring inspection images of parts captured by the inspection system, wherein the inspection images are associated with label information indicative of whether a given image corresponds to a non-defective or defective part, and further indicative of a defect type, for the inspection images corresponding to defective parts, training a binary classifier, using a first subset of the inspection images, to determine whether the inspection images correspond to non-defective or defective parts, the binary classifier using a first combination of neural network architecture and optimizer, the binary classifier being trained by iteratively updating weights associated with nodes thereof, training a multi-class classifier, using a second subset of the inspection images corresponding to defective parts, to determine the defect type in the inspection images previously determined by the binary classifier as corresponding to defective parts, the multi-class classifier using a second combination of neural network architecture and optimizer, the multi-class classifier being trained by iteratively updating weights associated with nodes thereof, and building, from the trained binary classifier and from the multi-class classifier, a defect classification model defined by a configuration file comprising the parameters of the first and second combinations of neural network architectures and optimizers and the updated weights of the nodes of each neural network architecture, the automatic defect classification model being thereby usable by the automated inspection system for detecting defective parts and for identifying defect types on the parts being inspected. 2. The computer-implemented method according to claim 1, wherein training the binary classifier further comprises an initial step of automatically exploring different combinations of neural network architecture and optimizer on an exploring subset of the inspection images, and wherein the first combination selected for the binary classifier corresponds to the combination that provides the highest accuracy in identifying non-defective from defective parts for a given number of epochs. 3. The computer-implemented method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein training the multi-class classifier further comprises an initial step of automatically exploring the different combinations of neural networks and optimizer on another exploring subset of the second subset of inspection images, and wherein the second combination of neural network architecture and optimizer corresponds to the combination that provides the highest accuracy in identifying the different defect types for a given number of epochs. 4. The computer-implemented method according to claim 3, wherein training the binary classifier further comprises automatically exploring different loss functions and different learning rate schedulers, and wherein the first combination is further defined by automatically selecting a loss function and a learning rate scheduler that provides, together with the neural network architecture and optimizer, the highest accuracy in detecting non-defective from defective parts for the given number of epochs, the configuration file of the defect classification model further comprising parameters from the selected loss function and learning rate scheduler of the binary classifier. 5. The computer-implemented method according to claim 4, wherein training the multi class classifier further comprises automatically exploring the different loss functions and the learning rate schedulers, and wherein the second combination is further defined by automatically selecting a loss function and a learning rate scheduler that provides, together with the neural network architecture and the optimizer, the highest accuracy in identifying the defect types for the given number of epochs, the configuration file of the defect classification model further comprising parameters from the selected loss function and learning rate scheduler of the multi-class classifier. 6. The computer-implemented method according to claims 4 or 5, wherein the updated weights and the parameters of the selected neural network architectures, optimizers, loss functions and learning rate schedulers are packaged in the configuration file that loadable by the automated inspection system. 7. The computer-implemented method according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the different neural network architectures comprise at least one of : ResNet34, NesNet50, ResNet101, ResNet152, WideResNet50, WideResNet101, lncptionV3 and InceptionResNet. 8. The computer-implemented method according to any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the different optimizers comprise at least one of: Adam and SGD optimizers. 9. The computer-implemented method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the different loss functions comprise at least one of: cross entropy and Nil loss functions. 10. The computer-implemented method according to any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the different rate learning schedulers comprise at least one of: decay and cyclical rate schedulers. 11. The computer-implemented method according to any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the parts comprise at least one of: semiconductor packages, wafers, side-single PCBs, double-side PCBs, multilayer PCBs and substrates. 12. The computer-implemented method according to any one of claims 1 to 11, wherein the multi-class classifier is trained to detect the defect types comprising one or more of: under plating, foreign material, incomplete parts, cracks, smudges, abnormal circuits, resist residue, deformation, scratches, clusters and metal film residue. 13. The computer-implemented method according to any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein acquiring the inspection images comprises capturing, through a graphical user interface, a selection of one or more image folders wherein the inspection images are stored. 14. The computer-implemented method according to claim 13, wherein training of the binary and multi-class classifiers is initiated in response to an input made through a graphical user interface. 15. The computer-implemented method according to claim 14, wherein the training of the binary and multi-class classifiers is controlled, via an input captured through the graphical user interface, to pause, abort or resume the training. 16. The computer-implemented method according to any one of claims 1 to 15, comprising validating whether the overall number of inspection images is sufficient to initiate the training of the binary classifier, and if so, whether the number of inspection images associated with each defect type is sufficient to initiate the training of the multi-class classifier, whereby the training the multi-class classifier is initiated only for defect types for which there are a sufficient number of inspection images. 17. The computer-implemented method according to claim 16, comprising increasing the number of inspection images of a given defect type, when the number of inspection images associated with the given defect type is insufficient, using data augmentation algorithms. 18. The computer-implemented method according to any one of claims 1 to 17, comprising automatically splitting, for each of the first and the second subsets, the inspection images into at least a training dataset and a validation dataset, prior to training the binary and multi-class classifier, the training dataset being used during training to set initial parameters of the first and the second combinations of the neural network architecture and optimizer, the validation dataset being used to adjust the weights of the nodes during the training of the binary and multi-class classifiers. 19. The computer-implemented method according to claim 18, comprising further automatically splitting the inspection images into a test dataset to confirm the parameters and adjusted weights of the first and second combinations, once the binary and multi-class classifiers have been trained. 20. The computer-implemented method according to any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein the number of inspection images used to train the binary and multi-class classifiers at each training iteration is dynamically adapted as a function of the available physical resources of the processor performing the training. 21. The computer-implementation method according to any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the number of inspection images passed at each iteration through the binary and multi-class classifiers are bundled in predetermined batch sizes which are tested until an acceptable batch size can be handled by the processor. 22. The computer-implemented method according to claim 21, wherein the training of the binary and multi-class classifiers is performed by feeding the inspection images to the classifiers in subsequent batches, and wherein the number of inspection images in each batch is dynamically adjusted as a function of an availability of processing resources. 23. The computer-implemented method according to any one of claims 1 to 22, wherein acquiring the inspection images comprises scanning an image server and displaying on a graphical user interface a representation of a folder architecture comprising a machine identifier, a customer identifier, a recipe identifier and a lot or device identifier, for selection by a user. 24. The computer-implemented method according to any one of the previous claims, comprising verifying whether the inspection images have already been stored on a training server prior to copying the inspection images to the training server. 25. An automated inspection system for generating, via machine learning, an automatic defect classification model for the inspection of semiconductor and/or Printed Circuit Board (PCB) parts, the system comprising: one or more dedicated servers, including processor(s) and data storage, the data storage having stored thereon: an acquisition module for acquiring inspection images of the semiconductor and/or Printed Circuit Board (PCB) parts, wherein the inspection images are associated with label information indicative of whether a given image corresponds to a non defective or defective part, and further indicative of a defect type, for the inspection images corresponding to defective parts; a training application comprising: o a binary classifier that is trainable, using a first subset of the inspection images, to determine whether the inspection images correspond to non defective or defective parts, by iteratively updating weights of nodes of the binary classifier, the binary classifier using a first combination of neural network architecture and optimizer, o a multi-class classifier that is trainable, using a second subset of the inspection images corresponding to defective parts, to determine the defect type in the inspection images previously determined by the binary classifier as corresponding to defective parts, by iteratively updating weights of nodes of the multi-class classifier, the multi-class classifier using a second combination of neural network architecture and optimizer; the training application comprising algorithms to generate, from the trained binary classifier and from the trained multi-class classifier, a defect classification model defined by a configuration file comprising the parameters of the first and second combinations of neural network architecture and optimizer and the updated weights of the nodes of each neural network architecture, the automatic defect classification model being thereby usable by the automated inspection system for detecting defects on additional parts being inspected. 26. The automated inspection system according to claim 25, wherein the data storage further stores an exploration module, a first set of different neural network architectures and a second set of optimizers, the exploration module being configured to explore different combinations of neural network architectures and optimizers on an exploring subset of the inspection images for training the binary classifier, the exploration module being further configured to select the first combination of neural network architecture and optimizer for the binary classifier that provides the highest accuracy in detecting non-defective from defective parts for a given number of epochs. 27. The automated inspection system according to the claim 26, wherein the exploration module is further configured to explore different combinations of neural and optimizers on the exploring subset of the inspection images for training the multi class classifier, the exploration module being further configured select the second combination of neural network architecture and an optimizer for the multi-class classifier that provides the highest accuracy in identifying defect types for a given number of epochs. 28. The automated inspection system according to any one of claims 25 to 27, comprising a graphical user interface, allowing a user to select one or more image folders wherein the inspection images are stored and to initiate, in response to an input made through the graphical user interface, the generation of the automatic defect classification model. 29. The automated inspection system according to any one of claims 25 to 28, comprising a database for storing the inspection images of parts captured by the inspection system, and for storing the label information indicative of whether a given image corresponds to a non-defective or defective part, and further indicative of a defect type, for the inspection images corresponding to defective parts. 30. The automated inspection system according to any one of claims 25 to 29, wherein the data storage of the one or more dedicated servers further store a pre-processing module, for validating whether the overall number of inspection images is sufficient to initiate the training of the binary and multi-class classifiers, and for copying the images to the database and processing the images, such as by using data augmentation algorithms. 31. title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR MANUFACTURING QUALITY CONTROL USING AUTOMATED VISUAL INSPECTION; SYSTÈME ET PROCÉDÉ DE CONTRÔLE QUALITÉ DE FABRICATION UTILISANT UNE INSPECTION VISUELLE AUTOMATISÉE
abstract:
A system and method for automated visual inspection is provided herein. The method includes providing an inspection image of the article to an object detection model trained to detect at least one defect type in an input image and generating object location data identifying a location of a detected object in the inspection image; comparing the inspection image to a golden sample image to identify an artifact in the inspection image corresponding to a difference between the inspection image and the golden sample image, wherein the artifact is defined by artifact location data describing a location of the artifact in the inspection image; and determining whether the artifact location data matches the object location data according to predetermined match criteria.

L'invention concerne un système et un procédé d'inspection visuelle automatisée. Le procédé comprend la fourniture d'une image d'inspection de l'article à un modèle de détection d'objet entraîné pour détecter au moins un type de défaut dans une image d'entrée et la production de données d'emplacement d'objet identifiant un emplacement d'un objet détecté dans l'image d'inspection ; la comparaison de l'image d'inspection avec une image d'échantillon témoin pour identifier un artefact dans l'image d'inspection correspondant à une différence entre l'image d'inspection et l'image d'échantillon témoin, l'artefact étant défini par des données d'emplacement d'artefact décrivant un emplacement de l'artefact dans l'image d'inspection ; et la détermination du fait que les données d'emplacement d'artefact correspondent aux données d'emplacement d'objet selon des critères de correspondance prédéterminés.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR AUGMENTED INTELLIGENCE IN DENTAL FILE SEGMENTATION; SYSTÈME ET PROCÉDÉ POUR UNE INTELLIGENCE AMPLIFIÉE DANS UNE SEGMENTATION DE FICHIER DENTAIRE
abstract:
A computer-implemented system and method of generating two-dimensional (2D) descriptors for three-dimensional (3D) dental objects. The system comprises an indexed slicer to receive a mesh file of a dental object and slice the mesh into a plurality of slices, each slice comprising a cross-sectional boundary of the dental object and a radial encoder assigning an indexing centroid and measuring a plurality of rays from the indexing centroid to the cross-sectional boundary. The 3D mesh file can then be stored as a 2D descriptor matrix in a dental descriptor database. Dental objects can be searched in the descriptor database based on their 2D descriptor matrixes and matched to other similar dental objects, preferably in a graphical processing unit, for use in anomaly detection, diagnostics, and prosthetic design.

Système mis en œuvre par ordinateur et procédé de génération de descripteurs bidimensionnels (2D) pour des objets dentaires tridimensionnels (3D). Le système comprend un segment indexé destiné à recevoir un fichier de maillage d'un objet dentaire et à segmenter le maillage en une pluralité de tranches, chaque tranche comprenant une limite de section transversale de l'objet dentaire et un codeur radial attribuant un centroïde d'indexation et mesurant une pluralité de rayons à partir du centroïde d'indexation jusqu'à la limite de section transversale. Le fichier de maillage 3D peut ensuite être stocké sous la forme d'une matrice de descripteurs 2D dans une base de données de descripteurs dentaires. Des objets dentaires peuvent être recherchés dans la base de données de descripteurs sur la base de leurs matrices de descripteurs 2D et mis en correspondance avec d'autres objets dentaires similaires, de préférence dans une unité de traitement graphique, destinée à être utilisée dans la détection d'anomalies, le diagnostic et la conception de prothèses.

title: TEXTURE MAPPING TO POLYGONAL MODELS FOR INDUSTRIAL INSPECTIONS; MAPPAGE DE TEXTURE SUR DES MODÈLES POLYGONAUX POUR INSPECTIONS INDUSTRIELLES
abstract:
System and method that includes: capturing an image of a manufactured part using an image sensing device, wherein an actual pose of the manufactured part relative to the image sensing device in the image can vary from an expected pose of the manufactured part relative to the image sensing device; estimating an optimal pose that represents a transformation of the expected pose to the actual pose based on (i) the image, (ii) initial pose data that indicates the expected pose, and (iii) a 3D model that models a model part that corresponds to the manufactured part as a set of polygonal facets; mapping texture data from the image to facets of the 3D model based on the optimal pose to generate a textured 3D model corresponding to the optimal pose; and rendering a virtual image for a further pose of the manufactured part based on the textured 3D model.

L'invention concerne un système et un procédé qui consistent à : capturer une image d'une pièce fabriquée à l'aide d'un dispositif de détection d'image, une pose réelle de la pièce fabriquée par rapport au dispositif de détection d'image dans l'image pouvant varier par rapport à une pose attendue de la pièce fabriquée par rapport au dispositif de détection d'image ; estimer une pose optimale qui représente une transformation de la pose attendue vers la pose réelle sur la base (i) de l'image, (ii) des données de pose initiale qui indiquent la pose attendue, et (iii) d'un modèle 3D qui modélise une pièce de modèle qui correspond à la pièce fabriquée sous la forme d'un ensemble de facettes polygonales ; mapper des données de texture de l'image sur des facettes du modèle 3D sur la base de la pose optimale pour générer un modèle 3D texturé correspondant à la pose optimale ; et obtenir un rendu d'une image virtuelle pour une autre pose de la pièce fabriquée sur la base du modèle 3D texturé.
claims: CLAIMS1. A computer implemented method comprising: capturing an image of a manufactured part using an image sensing device, wherein an actual pose of the manufactured part relative to the image sensing device in the image can vary from an expected pose of the manufactured part relative to the image sensing device; estimating an optimal pose that represents a transformation of the expected pose to the actual pose based on (i) the image, (ii) initial pose data that indicates the expected pose, and (iii) a 3D model that models a model part that corresponds to the manufactured part as a set of polygonal facets; mapping texture data from the image to facets of the 3D model based on the optimal pose to generate a textured 3D model corresponding to the optimal pose; and rendering a virtual image for a further pose of the manufactured part based on the textured 3D model. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein estimating the optimal pose comprises: detecting edges of the manufactured part from the image; projecting model edges from the 3D model, corresponding to the expected pose; determining visible model edges based on the projected model edges; mapping the detected edges to the visible model edges; determining transform data for transforming coordinates in the image to coordinates in the 3D model based on the mapping of the detected edges to the visible model edges; computing the optimal pose based on the transform data. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2 comprising classifying visibilities of the facets based on the optimal pose, wherein mapping texture data from the image to facets of the 3D model comprises selectively mapping the texture data to only some of the facets based on the visibility classifications of the facets. 4. The method of claim 3 wherein classifying visibilities of the facets comprises classifying the facets as one of visible, partially visible and non-visible by predicting an extent of visibility of the facets when imaged by an image sensing device at the optimal pose based on the 3D model. 5. The method of claim 4 predicting the extent of visibility of the facets comprises: rendering a first virtual image of the model part based on the 3D model, wherein rendered pixels of the first virtual image correspond to portions of the model part that are visible in the first virtual image from a perspective corresponding to the optimal pose; rendering a second virtual image of the model part wherein all facets of the model part are assumed to be visible; for each facet, determine the number of corresponding visible pixels in the first virtual image that map to the facet relative to the total number of pixels in the second virtual image that map to the facet, and: (i) when the relative number of visible pixels that map to the facet exceeds a first threshold then classify the facet as "visible"; (ii) when the relative number of visible pixels falls below a second threshold then classify the facet as "non-visible"; (iii) when the relative number of visible pixels falls between the first and second thresholds then classify the facet as "partially visible". 6. The method of anyone of claims 3 to 5 wherein selectively mapping the texture data to only some of the facets comprises: determining texture coordinates that map respective groups of pixel values from the image to respective facets that have been identified as visible or partially visible. 7. The method of claim 6 comprising adding the texture coordinates to the 3D model to generate the textured 3D model. 8. The method of claims 1 or 2 wherein mapping the texture data from the image to the facets of the 3D model comprises determining texture coordinates that map respective groups of pixels from the image to respective facets; and rendering the virtual image for a pose other than the optimal pose comprises managing occlusions of the manufactured part included in the image through rendering-based routines that incorporate shadow-mapping. 9. The method of claim 1 or 2 wherein rendering the virtual image comprises: rendering a first virtual depth buffer image for the textured 3D model corresponding to the optimal pose; rendering a second virtual depth buffer image for the textured 3D part model corresponding to the further pose; and performing shadow mapping based on a comparison of corresponding pixels of the first virtual depth buffer image and the second virtual depth buffer image, wherein rendering the virtual image is based on the shadow mapping. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9 further comprising providing the rendered virtual image to a classification function that is configured to output a prediction for the manufactured part based on the rendered virtual image. 11. The method of claim 10 wherein the classification function is configured to output a quality prediction for the manufactured part. 12. The method of claim 10 or 11 comprising rendering, for the image of the manufactured part, a plurality of virtual images for a plurality of further poses of the manufactured part based on the textured 3D model, and training a machine learning model to perform the classification function using a training set of the plurality of virtual images. 13. A system, comprising: an image sensor device for capturing the image the manufactured part at a predetermined location in an industrial process, the image comprising a two dimensional array of pixels each having an assigned texture value; one or more processing units that each comprise a processing device and a storage storing instructions for the processing device, the instructions collectively configuring the one or more processing units to perform the method of any one of claims 1 to 12. 14. A system comprising: an image sensing device that is positioned with respect to an image capture location in an industrial process; an image processing module configured to process an image of a manufactured part that is captured by the image sensing device while the manufactured part is located at the image capture location, wherein an actual pose of the manufactured part relative to the image sensing device in the image can vary from an expected pose of the manufactured part relative to the image sensing device; the image processing module being configured to: estimate an optimal pose that represents a transformation of the expected pose to the actual pose based on (i) the image, (ii) initial pose data that indicates the expected pose, and (iii) a 3D model that models a model part that corresponds to the manufactured part as a set of polygonal facets; map texture data from the image to facets of the 3D model based on the optimal pose to generate a textured 3D model corresponding to the optimal pose; and render a virtual image for a further pose of the manufactured part relative to the image sensing device based on the textured 3D model. 15. A computer readable medium storing a set of non-transitory executable software instructions that, when executed by one or more processing units, configure the one or more processing units to perform the method of any one of claims 1 to 12.

title: SYSTEM, METHOD, AND COMPUTER DEVICE FOR AGGREGATE THRESHOLDING, ADAPTIVE CROPPING, AND CLASSIFICATION OF IMAGES FOR ANOMALY DETECTION IN MACHINE VISION APPLICATIONS; SYSTÈME, PROCÉDÉ ET DISPOSITIF INFORMATIQUE PERMETTANT UN SEUILLAGE GLOBAL, UN CADRAGE ADAPTATIF ET UNE CLASSIFICATION D'IMAGES DESTINÉES À UNE DÉTECTION D'ANOMALIES DANS DES APPLICATIONS DE VISION ARTIFICIELLE
abstract:
Systems and methods for visual inspection and anomaly detection are provided herein. An inspection image is compared to a golden sample image to identify an anomaly map. Aggregate thresholding is performed on the anomaly map to identify anomalies. Adaptive cropping is performed on the identified anomalies to obtain cropped images of the anomalies. The cropped images are provided to an image classification model which is a pseudo one-class classifier. The image classification model classifies the anomalies.

L'invention concerne des systèmes et des procédés d'inspection visuelle et de détection d'anomalies. Une image d'inspection est comparée à une image d'échantillon témoin de façon à identifier une carte des anomalies. Un seuillage global est effectué sur la carte des anomalies de façon à identifier des anomalies. Un cadrage adaptatif est effectué sur les anomalies identifiées de façon à obtenir des images cadrées des anomalies. Les images cadrées sont transmises à un modèle de classification d'images qui est un pseudo-classificateur de classe unique. Le modèle de classification d'images classe les anomalies.
