title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR RECOGNISING AND ANALYSING SURFACE DEFECTS IN THREE-DIMENSIONAL OBJECTS HAVING A REFLECTIVE SURFACE, IN PARTICULAR MOTOR VEHICLE BODIES; PROCEDE ET DISPOSITIF DE RECONNAISSANCE ET D'ANALYSE DE DEFAUTS DE SURFACE D'OBJETS TRIDIMENSIONNELS PRESENTANT UNE SURFACE REFLECHISSANTE, EN PARTICULIER DE CARROSSERIES DE VEHICULE AUTOMOBILE
abstract:
The present invention relates to a method for recognising and analysing surface defects (OF) in three-dimensional objects (5) having a reflective surface (51), in particular motor vehicle bodies, in which method the surface defects (OF) are identified using the evaluation of at least one image, recorded by at least one camera (3) in the form of a raster image of pixels, of an illumination pattern (21) projected by at least one first illumination device (2) onto at least one part of the surface (51) using a two-dimensional raster coordinate system. The invention also relates to a device (1) therefor. The invention advantageously allows surface defects (OF) to be identified exclusively using two-dimensional image information with the aid of image processing algorithms without the need for "environmental parameters", and complex geometric calculations can advantageously be omitted. As a result, the solution according to the invention is fast and robust and can be carried out using differently designed first illumination devices (2), which makes it suitable in particular for mobile applications, for example as a hand-held module. It is also made possible for the method according to the invention to be optimised by a "deep learning" strategy.

La présente invention concerne un procédé et un dispositif de reconnaissance et d'analyse de défauts de surface (OF) d'objets tridimensionnels (5) présentant une surface réfléchissante (51), en particulier de carrosseries de véhicule automobile, selon lequel les défauts de surface (OF) sont identifiés au moyen d'un système de coordonnées matricielles bidimensionnel sur la base de l'évaluation d'au moins une image, enregistrée par au moins une caméra (3) sous la forme d'un graphique matriciel composé de pixels, d'un motif d'éclairage (21) projeté par au moins un premier dispositif d'éclairage (2) sur au moins une partie de la surface (51). L'invention concerne également un dispositif (1) afférent. L'invention permet avantageusement d'identifier les défauts de surface (OF) exclusivement sur la base d'informations d'image bidimensionnelles au moyen d'algorithmes de traitement d'images, aucun « paramètre environnemental » n'étant nécessaire et des calculs géométriques complexes pouvant être évités. La solution selon l'invention est de ce fait rapide et fiable, et peut être mise en uvre en combinant de premiers dispositifs d'éclairage (2) de conception différente, ce qui la prédestine en particulier à des applications mobiles, par exemple sous la forme d'un module à main. Il est en outre possible d'optimiser le procédé selon l'invention par une stratégie « d'apprentissage profond ».

title: IMAGE SAMPLING FOR VISUAL INSPECTION; ECHANTILLONNAGE D'IMAGE POUR INSPECTION VISUELLE
abstract:
A method for sampling images includes receiving a first image set generated by automated imaging equipment during a first inspection period, and storing in a memory an image library that initially consists of the first image set. A plurality of new image sets is then sequentially received (302) during respective inspection periods. While the new image sets are received (302), the image library stored in the memory is updated. Updating the image library includes, for each new image set, adding to the image library a certain number of images distributed among the new image set and removing from the image library the same number of images distributed among a current instance of the image library (308). The number of overwritten images in the image library decreases from one inspection period to the next.

L'invention ?concerne? ?un? procédé d'échantillonnage d'images qui comprend la réception d'un premier ensemble d'images généré par un équipement d'imagerie automatisé pendant une première période d'inspection, et le stockage dans une mémoire d'une bibliothèque d'images qui est initialement constituée du premier ensemble d'images. Une pluralité de nouveaux ensembles d'images sont ensuite reçus séquentiellement (302) pendant des périodes d'inspection respectives. Pendant que les nouveaux ensembles d'images sont reçus (302), la bibliothèque d'images stockée dans la mémoire est mise à jour. La mise à jour de la bibliothèque d'images comprend, pour chaque nouvel ensemble d'images, l'ajout à la bibliothèque d'images d'un certain nombre d'images distribuées parmi le nouvel ensemble d'images et la suppression, de la bibliothèque d'images, du même nombre d'images distribuées parmi une instance courante de la bibliothèque d'images (308). Le nombre d'images écrasées dans la bibliothèque d'images diminue d'une période d'inspection à la suivante.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR PROCESS SHAPING; SYSTEME ET PROCEDE DE MISE EN FORME DE PROCESSUS
abstract:
A system for process shaping in a retail store environment comprises a video generation and processing component, a data source integration and aggregation component for aggregating and integrate information received from various sources, a process sensing component for generating one or more continuous processes, a process aggregator and weighing component for aggregating the one or more continuous processes into a merged weighted process, a proof of problem and value component for determining one or more process variations, a ripple effect analyser for sending one or more nudging messages to the retail store environment, and a gamified feedback algorithm component for communicating a nudging action corresponding to a nudging message, to one or more entities in the retail store environment.

L'invention concerne un système de mise en forme de processus dans un environnement de magasin de détail qui comprend un composant de génération et de traitement de vidéo, un composant d'intégration et d'agrégation de source de données pour agréger et intégrer des informations reçues de diverses sources, un composant de détection de processus pour générer un ou plusieurs processus continus, un agrégateur de processus et un composant de pesage pour agréger le ou les processus continus en un processus pondéré fusionné, une preuve de problème et un composant de valeur pour déterminer une ou plusieurs variations de processus, un analyseur de répercussions pour envoyer un ou plusieurs messages d'incitation à l'environnement de magasin de vente au détail, et un composant d'algorithme de rétroaction ludique pour communiquer une action d'incitation correspondant à un message d'incitation, à une ou plusieurs entités dans l'environnement de magasin de vente au détail.
claims: CLAIMS 1. title: VIRTUAL REALITY SIMULATION AND METHOD; SIMULATION DE REALITE VIRTUELLE ET PROCEDE
abstract:
One aspect of the present disclosure include a method and system for providing an interactive virtual reality simulation for virtual reality training. A headset, controllers, and/or one or more sensors communicate with a processor to display the interactive virtual reality simulation on a user display within the headset. The interactive virtual reality training for use in facilitating virtually reality simulations including a microscope simulation, a bacteria streaking simulation, and/or a visually inspecting containers simulation.

Selon un aspect, la présente invention concerne un procédé et un système qui permettent d'obtenir une simulation de réalité virtuelle interactive pour un apprentissage de réalité virtuelle. Un casque d'écoute, des contrôleurs et/ou un ou plusieurs capteurs communiquent avec un processeur pour afficher la simulation de réalité virtuelle interactive sur un dispositif d'affichage d'utilisateur dans le casque d'écoute. L'apprentissage interactif de réalité virtuelle est destiné à être utilisé pour faciliter des simulations de réalité virtuelle comprenant une simulation de microscope, une simulation de striation des bactéries et/ou une simulation d'inspection visuelle de contenants.

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR OBJECT IDENTIFICATION; SYSTEMES ET PROCEDES D'IDENTIFICATION D'OBJET
abstract:
A method for identifying and tracking objects includes: capturing one or more 3-D models of one or more objects in a scene using a three-dimensional (3-D) scanning system, the one or more 3-D models including color and geometry information of the one or more objects; and computing, by an analysis agent, one or more descriptors of the one or more 3-D models, each descriptor corresponding to a fixed-length feature vector; and retrieving metadata identifying the one or more objects based on the one or more descriptors.

L'invention concerne un procédé permettant d'identifier et de suivre des objets, ledit procédé consistant : à capturer un ou plusieurs modèles 3D d'un ou de plusieurs objets dans une scène à l'aide d'un système de balayage en trois dimensions (3D), le ou les modèles 3D comprenant des informations de couleur et de géométrie du ou des objets; et à calculer, au moyen d'un agent d'analyse, un ou plusieurs descripteurs du ou des modèles 3D, chaque descripteur correspondant à un vecteur de caractéristique de longueur fixe; et à récupérer des métadonnées identifiant le ou les objets sur la base du ou des descripteurs.
claims: WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An object identification and tracking system comprising: a three-dimensional (3-D) scanning system configured to capture one or more 3-D models of one or more objects in a scene, the one or more 3-D models comprising color and geometry information of the one or more objects; and an analysis agent comprising a processor and memory, the memory storing instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to: receive the one or more 3-D models from the 3-D scanning system; compute one or more descriptors of the one or more 3-D models, each descriptor corresponding to a fixed-length feature vector; and retrieve metadata from a database, the instructions to retrieve the metadata comprising instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to search the database for one or more entries that are nearest-neighbors of the one or more descriptors, the metadata identifying the one or more objects based on the one or more descriptors. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the 3-D scanning system comprises one or more 3-D scanners, and each 3-D scanner comprising: a time-of-fiight depth camera; a structured light depth camera; or a stereo depth camera comprising: at least two infrared cameras; an infrared projector; and a color camera. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the 3-D scanning system is mounted on a basket containing the one or more objects. 4. The system of claim 3, wherein additions and removals of objects to and from the basket are computed frequently based on low level visual information acquired by the 3-D scanning system and stored as images. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the memory of the analysis agent further stores instructions that, when executed by the processor of the analysis agent, causes the processor to compute each of the one or more descriptors using a forward evaluation of a convolutional neural network. -65Date Reçue/Date Received 2022-08-15 6. The system of claim 5, wherein each of the one or more descriptors is computed by: rendering a plurality of 2-D views of a 3-D model of the one or more 3-D models; computing a plurality of feature vectors, each of the feature vectors corresponding to one of the 2-D views, by supplying each of the 2-D views of the 3-D model to the convolutional neural network; and pooling the feature vectors to generate a descriptor of the one or more descriptors, the descriptor corresponding to one of the one or more objects. 7. The system of claim 6, wherein at least one of the one or more 3-D models comprises at least two objects, and wherein the memory of the analysis agent further stores instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to: segment the at least one 3-D model into a plurality of segments; and compute a descriptor for each of the segments. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the metadata comprises one or more defect detection models, and wherein the memory further stores instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to: supply the one or more 3-D models to the one or more defect detection models; and classify the one or more 3-D models as being abnormal or clean. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more objects are located in a basket, the basket comprising a divider separating the basket into a plurality of bins, wherein the 3-D scanning system is configured to scan the plurality of bins, and wherein the analysis agent is configured to identify at most one object in each of the bins. 10. The system of claim 1, further comprising a check-in system configured to associate an identity of a customer with a basket comprising the one or more objects. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein the check-in system comprises: a badge reader; a biometric identification system; a Bluetooth transceiver; -66Date Reçue/Date Received 2022-08-15 a WiFi transceiver; a radio frequency identification (RFID) transceiver; or a near field communication (NFC) transceiver. 12. The system of claim 10, wherein the metadata associated with the one or more objects are matched to one or more marketing promotions, and wherein the customer is notified of the one or more marketing promotions in response to detecting the one or more objects in the basket 13. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more objects are components of a manufacturing kit, and wherein the memory of the analysis agent further stores instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to: retrieve a list of expected components of the manufacturing kit; compare the metadata identifying the one or more objects to the list of expected components; and detect differences between the list of expected components and the metadata of the one or more objects. 14. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more objects are components of an order to be shipped, and wherein the memory of the analysis agent further stores instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to: retrieve a shipping manifest comprising a list of one or more items expected to be included in the order to be shipped; compare the metadata identifying the one or more objects to the list of one or more items of the shipping manifest; and detect differences between the list of expected components and the metadata of the one or more objects. 15. title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR OBJECT IDENTIFICATION; SYSTEMES ET PROCEDES D'IDENTIFICATION D'OBJET
abstract:
A method for identifying and tracking objects includes: capturing one or more 3-D models of one or more objects in a scene using a three-dimensional (3-D) scanning system, the one or more 3-D models including color and geometry information of the one or more objects; and computing, by an analysis agent, one or more descriptors of the one or more 3-D models, each descriptor corresponding to a fixed-length feature vector; and retrieving metadata identifying the one or more objects based on the one or more descriptors.

L'invention concerne un procédé permettant d'identifier et de suivre des objets, ledit procédé consistant : à capturer un ou plusieurs modèles 3D d'un ou de plusieurs objets dans une scène à l'aide d'un système de balayage en trois dimensions (3D), le ou les modèles 3D comprenant des informations de couleur et de géométrie du ou des objets; et à calculer, au moyen d'un agent d'analyse, un ou plusieurs descripteurs du ou des modèles 3D, chaque descripteur correspondant à un vecteur de caractéristique de longueur fixe; et à récupérer des métadonnées identifiant le ou les objets sur la base du ou des descripteurs.

title: OPTIMIZING A SET-UP STAGE IN AN AUTOMATIC VISUAL INSPECTION PROCESS; OPTIMISATION D'UN ETAGE DE REGLAGE DANS UN PROCESSUS D'INSPECTION VISUELLE AUTOMATIQUE
abstract:
Embodiments of the invention provide a system and method for a visual inspection process, in which images of objects on an inspection line are automatically grouped into clusters based on the appearance of the object in the images. The clustered images create a broad database of images that can be used as a reference for inspected items having different appearances, which ensures that all parts and appearances of an inspected object can be identified and inspected, thereby increasing the success of defect detection and substantially minimizing false detection of defects.

L'invention porte, selon des modes de réalisation, sur un système et sur un procédé pour un processus d'inspection visuelle, selon lesquels des images d'objets sur une ligne d'inspection sont automatiquement regroupées sur la base de l'apparence de l'objet dans les images. Les images regroupées créent une grande base de données d'images qui peuvent être utilisées comme référence pour des articles inspectés ayant différents aspects, ce qui garantit que toutes les parties et tous les aspects d'un objet inspecté peuvent être identifiés et inspectés, ce qui permet d'augmenter le succès de la détection de défauts et de réduire considérablement le risque de fausse détection de défauts.
claims: CLAIMS 1. A visual inspection method comprising: detecting an object in a plurality of set-up images of an inspection line, in each set-up image the object conforming to a criterion; grouping the images into clusters according to values of the criterion; detecting the object in an inspection image of the inspection line; comparing the inspection image to one of the clusters according to a value of the criterion of the object in the inspection image; and detecting a defect on the object in the inspection image based on the comparison. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein grouping the images into clusters according to values of the criterion comprises assigning a first image to a first cluster and assigning a second image to a second cluster if a difference between a value of the criterion in the first image and a value of the criterion in the second image is above a threshold. 3. The method of claim 2 comprising grouping a pre-determined number of images into a cluster and wherein the threshold is determined based on the pre-determined number. 4. The method of claim 2 wherein the threshold is based on the criterion and on the object. 5. The method of claim 1 wherein the criterion comprises a spatial feature of the object in the images. 6. The method of claim 1 wherein the criterion comprises a visual feature of the object. 7. The method of claim 1 wherein the set-up images comprise images of defect-free objects and the inspection image comprises either a defect-free or defected object. 8. The method of claim 1 comprising displaying the clusters for user approval. 9. The process of claim 8 wherein displaying the clusters comprises displaying a representative of each cluster. 10. The method of claim 9 comprising displaying individual images of a particular cluster upon user request. CA 03117917 2021-04-27 WO 2020/100146 PCT/IL2019/051260 11. The process of claim 8 wherein the representative comprises an outline of the object. 12. The method of claim 11 wherein if the object is a 2D object, the outline loosely follows the contour of the object and wherein if the object is a 3D object, the outline closely follows the contour of the object. 13. The method of claim 1 comprising displaying the detected defects. 14. A visual inspection method comprising: assigning images of an object to a cluster based on appearance of the object in each image; determining if the cluster achieved a complete representation of the object; if a complete representation of the object is achieved, detecting defects on the object in a successive image by comparing the successive image to the images assigned to the cluster. 15. The method of claim 14 wherein determining if the cluster achieved a complete representation of the object comprises determining a number of images in the cluster. 16. The method of claim 14 wherein determining if the cluster achieved a complete representation of the object comprises comparing images assigned to the cluster to each other. 17. The method of claim 14 wherein determining if the cluster achieved a complete representation of the object comprises determining if each image assigned to the cluster can be used as a distortion-less reference to all other images assigned to the cluster. 18. The method of claim 14 wherein if a complete representation of the object is not achieved, then the method comprises using a successive image assigned to the cluster to update a database of reference images. 19. The method of claim 18 comprising displaying the cluster to a user for approval. 20. The method of claim 14 comprising retroactively detecting defects in images assigned to the cluster. 21. A system for visual inspection, the system comprising: a processor in communication with a display, the processor to: CA 03117917 2021-04-27 WO 2020/100146 PCT/IL2019/051260 receive an image of an object on an inspection line; detect the object in the image; assign the image to a cluster based on a spatial feature of the object in the image; determine a status of the cluster; and cause display of a representative of the cluster and an indication of the status of the cluster. 22. The system of claim 21 wherein the indication of the status of the cluster comprises a visual characteristic that is different for each different status. 23. The system of claim 22 wherein the visual characteristic comprises color. 24. The system of claim 21 wherein the processor is to display a first visual characteristic when the object in the image is detected and a second visual characteristic to indicate the status of the cluster. 25. A visual inspection method comprising: assigning an image of an object to a cluster based on appearance of the object in the image; based on a determination if the cluster achieved a complete representation of the object, determine a status of the cluster, determining whether or not to apply an inspection algorithm on the image based on the status of the cluster, thereby achieving inspection of some objects before all clusters achieved complete representation of the object. 26. A system for visual inspection, the system comprising: a processor to receive set-up images and inspection images of an object, the processor to: group the set-up images and inspection images into a cluster based on appearance of the object in each image; compare an inspection image to the set up images grouped to the cluster; detect a defect on the object in the inspection image based on the comparison; and cause display of the detected defect.

title: SENSOR SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR FACILITY OPERATION MANAGEMENT; SYSTEMES ET METHODES DE CAPTEUR POUR LA GESTION DE L`EXPLOITATION D`UNE INSTALLATION
abstract: Aspects relate to systems and methods for the application of computer vision and sensor fusion to assist in the management and operation of a facility. For large facilities, many of the expenses and staffing requirements incurred such as energy, custodial duties, maintenance and security can scale with size rather than usage, and therefore be subject to gross inefficiencies. These challenges may arise from a lack of timely information available with which to make such optimizations and improvements. The approaches disclosed leverage recent advancements in computer vision technology to extract actionable information from raw sensor data collected through-out the facility. This information may be processed and applied in either an autonomous, semi-autonomous, or user driven approach to control and manage a number of processes occurring regularly within a facility, such as lighting, cleaning, and security.
claims: Claims What is claim is: 1. A sensor system for operation management comprising: a plurality of inputs to receive data from one or more sensors an interface to access stored prior information; and a processing unit, further comprising: data aggregation & storage module; computer vision processing module; post-processing & analytics module; wherein the processing unit generates actionable information using stored prior information and input data. 2. The system of claim 1 wherein the one or more sensors is selected from a list consisting of video camera, optical camera, thermal camera, acoustic or sound pressure sensor, olfactory sensor, and motion sensor. 3. The system of claim 1 wherein the actionable information is sent to a user interface or a control unit. 4. A computer implemented method, using a control system, to detect garbage and soiled areas in need of cleaning, the method comprising: receiving video and sensor data; pre-processing the received video and sensor data; detecting garbage in the foreground using a convolutional neural network (CNN) detector algorithm; detecting whether the background has changed using an anomaly detection algorithm; and if garbage is detected or the background has changed, identify the area and provide an alert notification to user. 5. The system of claim 4 wherein the anomaly detection algorithm monitors the imagery of the space for any differences in the background. Date Reçue/Date Received 2021-05-26 6. The system of claim 4 wherein the differences in the background include objects not moving to differentiate from transient people or objects moving through the scene. 7. The system of claim 4 wherein the step of detecting whether the background has changed uses an anomaly detection algorithm further comprising detecting damage or anomalous behavior indicative of an event requiring maintenance. 8. A computer implemented method, using a control system, to identify objects and surfaces at higher risk of contamination due to contact or proximity to an individual or an event, the method comprising: receiving video & sensor data; detecting contamination event using two or more convolutional neural network (CNN) detector algorithms; identifying area of contamination; and providing alert notification to user. 9. The method of claim 8 wherein the event is a person detector that analyzes estimated distance from or between various objects within a field of view in order to infer which objects are at higher risk of contamination. 10. The method of claim 8 further comprising using multiple cameras and angles to better estimate distances and locations. 11. The method of claim 8 wherein the event is an expulsion event. 12. The method of claim 11 wherein the expulsion event is selected from a list consisting of a cough, a sneeze and an excretion of bodily fluids. 13. The method of claim 11 further comprising the step of detecting body motion. 14. The method of claim 13 further comprising the step of creating a contamination vector in the direction of the detected expulsion event. Date Reçue/Date Received 2021-05-26 15. The method of claim 8 wherein the area of contamination is created to determine the risk of contamination. 16. A computer implemented method, using a control system, to determine control flow of people in a facility, the method comprising: receiving video & sensor data; monitoring the distribution of people throughout the facility using convolutional neural network (CNN) detector algorithms; counting the number of people; logging the number of people at predetermined time intervals; building a time series of people for each video camera; monitoring for temporal trends; and displaying information to the user. 17. The method of claim 15 wherein the step of monitoring for temporal trends further comprises monitoring anomalous behavior over time. 18. The method of claim 15 wherein the predetermined time interval is selected from a list including 5 minutes, 10 minutes, 30 minutes, 60 minutes, 2 hours, 24 hours and 48 hours. 19. A computer implemented method, using a control system, to determine the flow of people in a facility, the method comprising: receiving video & sensor data; analyzing video camera feeds to detect when a person enters or exits the field of view using a person detector algorithm; identifying the direction of travel; aggregating flow of people data to construct an estimate of the flow of people within the facility over time; and outputting data to a user interface. Date Reçue/Date Received 2021-05-26 20. A computer implemented method, using a control system, to autonomously detect occupancy of a space to activate sensor controls, the method comprising: receiving video and sensor data; receiving data from a convolutional neural network (CNN) detector; determining that occupancy of a space has changed based on data provided by the CNN detector; and providing alert notification to user or control unit. Date Reçue/Date Received 2021-05-26

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR FACILITATING CLONE SELECTION; SYSTEMES ET PROCEDES POUR FACILITER LA SELECTION DE CLONES
abstract:
A method for facilitating clone selection includes generating time-sequence images of a well containing a medium, including a first image and a later, second image. The method also includes detecting, by one or more processors analyzing the first image, one or more candidate objects depicted in the first image, and, for each of the candidate objects, determining whether the object is a single cell by analyzing an image of the object using a convolutional neural network. The method further includes detecting, by analyzing the second image with the processor(s), a cell colony depicted in the second image, and determining, by the processor(s), whether the colony was formed from only one cell based at least on whether each candidate object was determined to be a single cell. The method further includes generating, by the processor(s), output data indicating whether the colony was formed from only one cell.

L'invention concerne un procédé pour faciliter la sélection de clones qui consiste à générer des images en séquence temporelle d'un puits contenant un milieu, comprenant une première image et une seconde image ultérieure. Le procédé consiste également à détecter, par un ou plusieurs processeurs analysant la première image, un ou plusieurs objets candidats représentés dans la première image, et, pour chaque objet candidat, déterminer si l'objet est une cellule unique par analyse d'une image de l'objet à l'aide d'un réseau neuronal convolutif. Le procédé consiste en outre à détecter, par analyse de la seconde image avec le ou les processeurs, une colonie de cellules représentée dans la seconde image, et déterminer, par le ou les processeurs, si la colonie a été formée à partir d'une seule cellule au moins sur la base du fait que chaque objet candidat a été déterminé comme étant une cellule unique. Le procédé consiste en outre à générer, par le ou les processeurs, des données de sortie indiquant si la colonie a été formée à partir d'une seule cellule.
claims: What is claimed is: 1. A method for facilitating clone selection, the method comprising: generating, by a first imaging unit, a plurality of time-sequence images of a well containing a medium, the plurality of time-sequence images including a first time-sequence image generated at a first time and a second time-sequence image generated at a second time later than the first time; detecting, by one or more processors analyzing the first time-sequence image, one or more candidate objects depicted in the first time-sequence image; for each of the one or more candidate objects, determining, by the one or more processors analyzing an image of the candkiate object using a convolutional neural network, whether the candidate object is a single cell; detecting, by the one or more processors analyzing the second time-sequence image, a cell colony depicted in the second time-sequence image; determining, by the one or more processors, whether the cell colony was formed from only one cell based at least in part on whether each of the one or more candkiate objects was determined to be a single cell; and generating, by the one or more processors, output data indicating whether the cell colony was formed from only one cell. 2. The method of claim 3, wherein determining whether the first candidate object developed into the cell colony includes determining whether the third time-sequence image depicts an intermediate cell colony having one or both of (i) a smaller cell count than the cell colony, or (ii) a smaller size than that cell colony. title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR ANOMALY DETECTION IN SELF-CHECKOUT RETAIL ENVIRONMENTS; PROCEDE ET APPAREIL DE DETECTION D'ANOMALIES DANS DES ENVIRONNEMENTS DE VENTE AU DETAIL AVEC CAISSES EN LIBRE SERVICE
abstract:
A system for anomaly detection in a self-checkout environment, comprising a processing unit for receiving transaction data from a self-checkout terminal: characterising an activity based on a set of features extracted from the received transaction data; defining a plurality of active intervals for each characterised activity; determining a meta-feature vector for each defined active interval of the plurality of active intervals; comparing each meta feature vector with a predefined set of vectors; and determining an anomaly based on the comparison.

L'invention concerne un système de détection d'anomalies dans un environnement avec caisses en libre service, comportant une unité de traitement destinée à recevoir des données de transactions en provenance d'un terminal de caisse en libre service pour: caractériser une activité d'après un ensemble de caractéristiques extraites des données de transactions reçues; définir une pluralité d'intervalles actifs pour chaque activité caractérisée; déterminer un vecteur de méta-caractéristiques pour chaque intervalle actif défini de la pluralité d'intervalles actifs; comparer chaque vecteur de méta-caractéristiques à un ensemble prédéfini de vecteurs; et déterminer une anomalie sur la base de la comparaison.

title: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR DEFECT DETECTION IN IMAGE DATA OF A TARGET COATING; PROCEDE ET SYSTEME DE DETECTION DE DEFAUT DANS DES DONNEES D'IMAGE D'UN REVETEMENT CIBLE
abstract:
The present invention relates to a computer-implemented method, comprising: - obtaining, using an image capturing device, at least one digital image, each digital image being obtained at a different angle with respect to a surface of a target coating; - performing, using an electronic computer processor in an operative connection with at least one filtering unit, a first image analysis on the obtained at least one image to determine at least one bright region within the at least one image by isolating image foreground data from image background data; - performing, using the processor, a blob analysis to determine at least one corrupt area within the at least one bright region; and if at least one corrupt area is found, - masking out the at least one corrupt area for further analysis of the at least one image, or - rejecting the at least one image, and/or - initiate a repetition of the image capturing. Further, a respective system and a respective computer-readable medium are provided.

La présente invention porte sur un procédé mis en uvre par ordinateur, qui consiste : - à obtenir, à l'aide d'un dispositif de capture d'image, au moins une image numérique, chaque image numérique étant obtenue à un angle différent par rapport à une surface d'un revêtement cible ; - à effectuer, à l'aide d'un processeur informatique électronique dans une connexion fonctionnelle avec au moins une unité de filtrage, une première analyse d'image sur la ou les images obtenues pour déterminer au moins une région lumineuse à l'intérieur de la ou des images par isolation de données d'avant-plan d'image des données d'arrière-plan d'image ; - à effectuer, à l'aide du processeur, une analyse de tache pour déterminer au moins une zone corrompue à l'intérieur de la ou des régions lumineuses ; et si au moins une zone corrompue est trouvée, - à masquer la ou les zones corrompues en vue d'une analyse ultérieure de la ou des images, ou - à rejeter la ou les images, et/ou - à initier une répétition de la capture d'image. L'invention concerne en outre un système et un support lisible par ordinateur respectifs.
claims: 1. A computer-implemented method, comprising: - obtaining, using an image capturing device, at least one digital image of a target coating, each digital image being obtained at a different angle with respect to a surface of the target coating; - performing, using an electronic computer processor in an operative connection with at least one filtering unit, a first image analysis on the obtained at least one digital image to determine at least one bright region within the at least one digital image by isolating image foreground data from image background data; - performing, using the processor, a blob analysis to determine at least one corrupt area within the at least one bright region; wherein the blob analysis comprises searching in the image foreground data obtained from the first image analysis for blobs with a big aspect ratio compared to the majority of residual blobs obtained from the image foreground data, wherein a second threshold value is defined and an aspect ratio of each blob obtained from the image foreground data is compared with the second threshold value and a blob is identified as corrupt area when its aspect ratio is equal or greater than the second threshold value, wherein the second threshold value for the blob aspect ratios is set to more than 3; and if at least one corrupt area is found, - masking out the at least one corrupt area for further analysis of the respective digital image, for further image processing as the at least one corrupt area is removed, or - rejecting the respective digital image and initiating a repetition of the image capturing in order to obtain a new digital image of the target coating. □ate Reçue/Date Received 2023-01-12 2. The method according to claim 1, further comprising performing a second image analysis on the at least one masked image to identify physical property attributes of the target coating. 3. The method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the first image analysis is chosen as an image segmentation. 4. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the blob analysis comprises searching in the image foreground data obtained from the first image analysis for blobs with specific pre-defined geometric properties. 5. The method according to claim 4, wherein the blob analysis comprises searching in the image foreground data obtained from the first image analysis for blobs with a big surface area compared to the majority of residual blobs obtained from the image foreground data, wherein a first threshold value is defined and a surface area of each blob obtained from the image foreground data is compared with the first threshold value and a blob is identified as corrupt area when its surface area is equal or greater than the first threshold value. 6. title: ANOMALY DETECTION METHOD, SYSTEM AND COMPUTER READABLE MEDIUM; PROCEDE DE DETECTION D'ANOMALIE, SYSTEME ET SUPPORT LISIBLE PAR ORDINATEUR
abstract:
A method of detecting an anomaly is provided, including dividing each frame of a video stream into a plurality of cells; in each cell formulate statistics based on metadata generated for the frame, the metadata related to presence of an object in the cell, velocity of objects in the cell, direction of motion of objects in the cell, and classification of objects in the cell; and using the formulated statistics to determine when the anomalous activity has occurred in one of the cells of the plurality of cells.

L'invention concerne un procédé permettant de détecter une anomalie, ledit procédé consistant à : diviser chaque trame d'un flux vidéo en une pluralité de cellules ; dans chaque cellule, formuler des statistiques d'après les métadonnées générées pour la trame, les métadonnées étant associées à la présence d'un objet dans la cellule, à la vitesse d'objets dans la cellule, à la direction de mouvement d'objets dans la cellule et à la classification d'objets dans la cellule ; et utiliser les statistiques formulées pour déterminer quand l'activité anormale s'est produite dans l'une des cellules de la pluralité de cellules.

title: METHODS AND SYSTEMS FOR CRACK DETECTION USING A FULLY CONVOLUTIONAL NETWORK; PROCEDES ET SYSTEMES DE DETECTION DE FISSURES A L'AIDE D'UN RESEAU A CONVOLUTION COMPLETE
abstract:
Systems and methods for detecting cracks in a surface by analyzing a video, including an full-HD video, of the surface. The video contains successive frames, wherein individual frames of overlapping consecutive pairs of the successive frames have overlapping areas and a crack that appears in a first individual frame of a consecutive pair of the successive frames also appears in at least a second individual frame of the consecutive pair. A fully convolutional network (FCN) architecture implemented on a processing device is then used to analyze at least some of the individual frames of the video to generate crack score maps for the individual frames, and a parametric data fusion scheme implemented on a processing device is used to fuse crack scores of the crack score maps of the individual frames to identify cracks in the individual frames.

L'invention concerne également des systèmes et des procédés de détection de fissures dans une surface par analyse d'une vidéo, comprenant une vidéo pleine HD, de la surface. La vidéo contient des trames successives, des trames individuelles de paires consécutives se chevauchant des trames successives ayant des zones de chevauchement et une fissure qui apparaît dans une première trame individuelle d'une paire consécutive des trames successives apparaît également dans au moins une seconde trame individuelle de la paire consécutive. Une architecture de réseau à convolution complète (FCN) mise en uvre sur un dispositif de traitement est ensuite utilisée pour analyser au moins certaines des trames individuelles de la vidéo pour générer des cartes de score de fissure pour les trames individuelles, et un schéma de fusion de données paramétriques mis en uvre sur un dispositif de traitement est utilisé pour fusionner des scores de fissure des cartes de score de fissure des trames individuelles pour identifier des fissures dans les trames individuelles.

title: AUTOMATED MEDICAL IMAGE ANNOTATION AND ANALYSIS; ANNOTATION ET ANALYSE AUTOMATISEES D'IMAGES MEDICALES
abstract:
Systems and methods are provided for automatically marking locations within a radiograph of one or more dental pathologies, anatomies, anomalies or other conditions determined by automated image analysis of the radiograph by a number of different machine learning models. Image annotation data may be generated based at least in part on obtained results associated with output of the multiple machine learning models, where the image annotation data indicates at least one location in the radiograph and an associated dental pathology, restoration, anatomy or anomaly detected at the at least one location by at least one of the machine learning models. A user interface may present at least a portion of a radiograph's image data, along with display of visual bounding shapes appearing to be overlaid over the at least a portion of the image data to visually mark the presence and location of a given pathology or other condition.

L'invention concerne des systèmes et des procédés permettant de marquer automatiquement des emplacements dans une radiographie d'une ou de plusieurs pathologies, anatomies, anomalies ou autres conditions dentaires déterminées par une analyse d'image automatisée de la radiographie par un certain nombre de différents modèles d'apprentissage machine. Des données d'annotation d'image peuvent être générées sur la base, au moins en partie, des résultats obtenus associés à la sortie des multiples modèles d'apprentissage machine, les données d'annotation d'image indiquant au moins un emplacement dans la radiographie et une pathologie, une restauration, une anatomie ou une anomalie dentaire associée ayant été détectée au niveau du ou des emplacements par au moins l'un des modèles d'apprentissage machine. Une interface utilisateur peut présenter au moins une partie des données d'image d'une radiographie, conjointement avec l'affichage de formes de délimitation visuelle qui semblent être superposées sur la ou les parties des données d'image pour marquer visuellement la présence et l'emplacement d'une pathologie ou d'un autre état donné.

title: REMOTE MONITORING METHOD USING IMAGE PROCESSING INTELLIGENCE; PROCÉDÉ DE SURVEILLANCE À DISTANCE UTILISANT UNE INTELLIGENCE DE TRAITEMENT D'IMAGE
abstract:
A method of remote facilities monitoring for the detection of contamination, leaks or failures in petrochemical and related facilities. Remote cameras are used to capture images of equipment to be inspected, which are transmitted to a central server. A software component on the central server applies artificial intelligence and image processing methods to detect the presence of anomalies in the images for display to an operator and potential subsequent dispatch of in person follow-up. Parallel data streams from sensors at the remote sites can be used by the software on the server to enhance the level of confidence in anomaly detection. The server software uses an adaptive object-detection function to analyze image data and learns over time to provide enhanced detection of interest regions and failure conditions.

Il est décrit un procédé de surveillance d’installations à distance pour déterminer la présence de contaminations, de fuites ou de défaillances dans des installations pétrochimiques et des installations connexes. Des caméras de télésurveillance sont utilisées pour capturer des images d’un équipement à inspecter, qui sont transmises à un serveur central. Un composant logiciel sur le serveur central fait appel à l’intelligence artificielle et à des procédés de traitement d’images pour détecter la présence d’anomalies dans les images afin de générer un affichage pour un opérateur et de permettre une éventuelle répartition ultérieure de suivi en personne. Des flux de données parallèles provenant de capteurs au niveau des sites éloignés peuvent être utilisés par le logiciel sur le serveur pour améliorer le niveau de confiance dans la détection d’anomalies. Le logiciel serveur utilise une fonction de détection d’objets adaptative pour analyser des données d’images et apprend avec le temps à fournir une détection améliorée de régions d’intérêt et de conditions de défaillance.

title: PICTURE RECOGNIZING METHOD, DEVICE, COMPUTER EQUIPMENT AND STORAGE MEDIUM; METHODE DE RECONNAISSANCE D'IMAGE, DISPOSITIF, EQUIPEMENT INFORMATIQUE ET SUPPORT DE STOCKAGE
abstract: The present application relates to a picture recognizing method, comprising: obtaining a picture to be detected to which a current business process corresponds, the picture to be detected includes a current commodity list to which current commodities correspond; determining from the picture to be detected a commodity list region in which the current commodity list locates; clipping the commodity list region, and obtaining plural commodity list cells; determining the various commodity list cells as inputs of a target picture content recognition model, recognizing commodity list cells through the target picture content recognition model, and obtaining commodity list cell contents to which commodity list cells correspond; obtaining current commodity information corresponding to the current business process from a target business system; and determining any abnormal commodity list cell according to the current commodity information and the various commodity list cell contents, and marking the same in a detected picture.
claims: 1. A picture recognizing method, characterized in that the method comprises: obtaining a picture to be detected to which a current business process corresponds, wherein the picture to be detected includes a current commodity list to which current commodities correspond; determining from the picture to be detected a commodity list region in which the current commodity list locates; clipping the commodity list region, and obtaining a plurality of commodity list cells; determining the various commodity list cells as inputs of a target picture content recognition model, recognizing the various commodity list cells through the target picture content recognition model, and obtaining commodity list cell contents to which the various commodity list cells correspond; obtaining current commodity information corresponding to the current business process from a target business system; and determining any abnormal commodity list cell according to the current commodity information and the various commodity list cell contents, and marking the same in a detected picture. 2. The method according to Claim 1, characterized in that, prior to the step of obtaining a picture to be detected to which a current business process corresponds, the method comprises: obtaining a current business process starting instruction, and starting the current business process according to the current business process starting instruction; obtaining a current picture recognition On/Off status to which the current business process corresponds; and entering the step of obtaining a picture to be detected to which a current business process corresponds when it is detected that the current picture recognition On/Off status is On. Date Reçue/Date Received 2022-03-07 3. The method according to Claim 1, characterized in that the step of determining from the picture to be detected a commodity list region in which the current commodity list locates includes: obtaining a well-trained target object detection model, wherein the target object detection model is employed to recognize list regions in a picture; and inputting the picture to be detected in the target object detection model, performing object recognition on the picture to be detected through the target object detection model, and obtaining a commodity list region in which the current commodity list locates in the picture to be detected. 4. The method according to Claim 1, characterized in that the step of obtaining current commodity information corresponding to the current business process from a target business system includes: sending a commodity information obtaining request to the target business system, wherein the commodity information obtaining request includes a current business process identification; and receiving current commodity information matching with the current business process identification returned by the taiget business system according to the commodity information obtaining request. 5. The method according to Claim 1, characterized in that the step of determining any abnormal commodity list cell according to the current commodity information and the various commodity list cell contents, and marking the same in a detected picture includes: detecting whether there is commodity code information identical with a current commodity code in the current commodity information, wherein the current commodity code is a commodity code to which the current commodity corresponds; comparing, when it is detected that there is commodity code information identical with a current commodity code in the current commodity information, whether there is commodity information matching with the various commodity list cell contents in the current commodity information; Date Reçue/Date Received 2022-03-07 determining any commodity list cell content not matching with the current commodity information as an abnormal commodity list cell; and obtaining coordinate information of the abnormal commodity list cell, and marking in the detected picture according to the coordinate information. 6. The method according to Claim 5, characterized in that the method further comprises: determining, when it is detected that there is no commodity code information identical with a current commodity code in the current commodity information, the detected picture in the current business process as an abnormal detected picture, and ending the current business process. 7. The method according to Claim 5, characterized in that the step of comparing whether there is commodity information matching with the various commodity list cell contents in the current commodity information includes: detecting whether there is any commodity list empty cell content in the various commodity list cell contents; removing the commodity list cell content to which any commodity list empty cell content corresponds, and determining the remaining commodity list cell contents as intermediate commodity list cell contents; determining whether character strings to which the intermediate commodity list cell contents correspond are of a purely digital type; transforming the intermediate commodity list cell contents to which the purely digital type corresponds into target type commodity list cell contents; and comparing whether there is any commodity information matching with intermediate commodity list cell contents corresponding to a non-purely digital type and the target type commodity list cell contents in the current commodity information. 8. A picture recognizing device, characterized in that the device comprises: a to-be-detected picture obtaining module, for obtaining a picture to be detected to which a Date Reçue/Date Received 2022-03-07 current business process corresponds, wherein the picture to be detected includes a current commodity list to which current commodities correspond; a list region determining module, for determining from the picture to be detected a commodity list region in which the current commodity list locates; a list region clipping module, for clipping the commodity list region, and obtaining a plurality of commodity list cells; a list cell processing module, for determining the various commodity list cells as inputs of a target picture content recognition model, recognizing the various commodity list cells through the target picture content recognition model, and obtaining commodity list cell contents to which the various commodity list cells correspond; a current commodity information obtaining module, for obtaining current commodity information corresponding to the current business process from a target business system; and an abnormal detected picture marking module, for determining any abnormal commodity list cell according to the current commodity information and the various commodity list cell contents, and marking the same in a detected picture. 9. A computer equipment, comprising a memory, a processor, and a computer program stored on the memory and operable on the processor, characterized in that steps of the method according to any of Claims 1 to 7 are realized when the processor executes the computer program. 10. A computer-readable storage medium, storing thereon a computer program, characterized in that steps of the method according to any of Claims 1 to 7 are realized when the computer program is executed by a processor.

title: TARGETED APPLICATION OF DEEP LEARNING TO AUTOMATED VISUAL INSPECTION EQUIPMENT; APPLICATION CIBLEE D'UN APPRENTISSAGE PROFOND A UN EQUIPEMENT D'INSPECTION VISUELLE AUTOMATISEE
abstract:
In a method for enhancing accuracy and efficiency in automated visual inspection of vessels, a vessel containing a sample is oriented such that a line scan camera has a profile view of an edge of a stopper of the vessel. A plurality of images of the edge of the stopper is captured by the first line scan camera while spinning the vessel, where each image of the plurality of images corresponds to a different rotational position of the vessel. A two-dimensional image of the edge of the stopper is generated based on at least the plurality of images, and pixels of the two-dimensional image are processed, by one or more processors executing an inference model that includes a trained neural network, to generate output data indicative of a likelihood that the sample is defective.

Selon un procédé pour améliorer la précision et l'efficacité d'une inspection visuelle automatisée de récipients, un récipient contenant un échantillon est orienté de sorte qu'une caméra à balayage de lignes a une vue de profil d'un bord d'un bouchon du récipient. Une pluralité d'images du bord du bouchon sont capturées par la première caméra à balayage de lignes pendant que le récipient est tourné, chaque image de la pluralité d'images correspondant à une position de rotation différente du récipient. Une image bidimensionnelle du bord du bouchon est générée sur la base au moins de la pluralité d'images, et des pixels de l'image bidimensionnelle sont traités, par un ou plusieurs processeurs exécutant un modèle d'inférence qui comprend un réseau neuronal formé, pour générer des données de sortie indiquant une vraisemblance que l'échantillon est défectueux.

title: OFFLINE TROUBLESHOOTING AND DEVELOPMENT FOR AUTOMATED VISUAL INSPECTION STATIONS; DEPANNAGE ET DEVELOPPEMENT HORS LIGNE POUR STATIONS D'INSPECTION VISUELLE AUTOMATISEE
abstract:
In a method for replicating performance of an automated visual inspection (AVI) station, a mimic AVI station that performs one or more AVI functions of the AVI station is constructed. One or more container images are captured by an imaging system of the AVI station while a container is illuminated by an illumination system of the AVI station, and one or more additional container images are captured by a mimic imaging system of the mimic AVI station. The method also includes identifying, by one or more processors, one or more differences between the one or more additional container images and the one or more container images, generating, by the one or more processors, a visual indication of the difference(s) and/or one or more suggestions for modifying the mimic AVI station, and modifying the mimic AVI station based on the visual indication.

L'invention concerne un procédé de réplication des performances d'une station d'inspection visuelle automatisée (AVI), dans lequel est construite une station d'AVI d'imitation, qui effectue une ou plusieurs fonctions d'AVI de la station d'AVI. Une ou plusieurs images de récipient sont capturées par un système d'imagerie de la station d'AVI, tandis qu'un récipient est éclairé par un système d'éclairage de la station d'AVI, et une ou plusieurs images supplémentaires de récipient sont capturées par un système d'imagerie d'imitation de la station d'AVI d'imitation. Le procédé consiste également à identifier, par un ou par plusieurs processeurs, une ou plusieurs différences entre lesdites images supplémentaires de récipient et lesdites images de récipient ; à générer, par lesdits processeurs, une indication visuelle de la ou des différence(s) et/ou une ou plusieurs suggestions de modification de la station d'AVI d'imitation ; et à modifier la station d'AVI d'imitation en fonction de l'indication visuelle.

title: AUTOMATED VOLUMETRIC IMAGE CAPTURE OF AN OBJECT TO SUPPORT GENERAL VISUAL INSPECTION; ENREGISTREMENT AUTOMATISE D'IMAGE VOLUMETRIQUE D'UN OBJET A L'APPUI D'UNE INSPECTION VISUELLE GENERALE
abstract: Systems and methods provide technology for inspections including receiving image data from a sensor, the sensor operable to scan an object, where the object is a manufactured object, generating a 3D image record for the object based on the image data, comparing the 3D image record with a stored design model for the object and/or a stored baseline image record of the object, and generating findings data based on the comparing, where the findings data is indicative of a discrepancy identified between the 3D image record and the one or more of the stored design model for the object or the stored baseline image record of the object. The sensor can be mounted on a moving platform such as a robotic arm, a track mounted assembly or a drone. The technology can further include a motion or flight plan for moving the sensor relative to the object.

title: SYSTEMS, METHODS AND PROGRAMS FOR GENERATING DAMAGE PRINT IN A VEHICLE; SYSTEMES, PROCEDES ET PROGRAMMES POUR GENERER UNE EMPREINTE DE DOMMAGES DANS UN VEHICULE
abstract:
The disclosure relates to systems, methods and computer readable media for providing network-based identification, generation and management of a unique damage (finger)print of vehicle(s) by geodetic mapping of stable key points onto a ground truth 3D model of the vehicle, and vehicle parts - identified from the raw images using supervised and unsupervised machine learning. Specifically, the disclosure relates to System and methods for the generation of unique damage print on a vehicle that is obtained from captured images of the damaged vehicle, photogrammetrically localized to a specific vehicle part, and the computer programs enabling the method, the damage print configured to be used, for example, in fraud detection in insurance claims.

La présente invention concerne des systèmes, des procédés et des supports lisibles par ordinateur pour fournir, sur la base d'un réseau, une identification, une génération et une gestion d'une empreinte (digitale) unique de dommages d'un ou de plusieurs véhicules par un mappage géodésique de points clés stables sur un modèle 3D de réalité de terrain du véhicule, et de parties de véhicule - identifiées à partir des images brutes à l'aide d'un apprentissage automatique supervisé et non supervisé. Spécifiquement, la présente invention a trait à un système et à des procédés pour la génération d'une empreinte unique de dommages sur un véhicule qui est obtenue à partir d'images capturées du véhicule endommagé, localisée de manière photogrammétrique à une partie spécifique du véhicule, et aux programmes d'ordinateur permettant le procédé, l'empreinte de dommages étant configurée pour être utilisée, par exemple, pour une détection de fraude dans des demandes de règlement au titre de l'assurance.
claims: WO 2021/113279 PCT/US2020/062775 What is claimed: 1. A computerized networked method of generating a damage print on a vehicle, implementable in a system comprising: a backend management server (BMS), a user access terminal; a vehicle; a vehicle database; and a damage print database, wherein the BMS further comprises a network communication module configured to communicate with the communication network, and a central processing module (CPM), in communication with the communication module, the vehicle database and the damage print database, the CPM further comprises at least one processor in communication with a non-volatile memory storage device, storing thereon a processor readable media with a set of executable instructions, configured, when executed to cause the at least one processor to: receive images, and generate the damage print, the method comprising: a. receiving at least one image associated with the vehicle damage from the user; b. using the at least one image, identifying the vehicle; c. identifying at least one damages part in the vehicle; d. georeferencing the damage on the identified damaged vehicle part; e. registering the georeferenced damage to a 3D model of the identified vehicle; and f. generating a unique damage print. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein identifying the vehicle further comprises: identifying at least one of: make, model, trim, year and principal color. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising receiving from the user a plurality of images, each associated with the vehicle damage. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the plurality of images associated with the vehicle damage are captured at at least one of: a different distance, and a different angle relative to the damage. 5. The method of claim 2, wherein the step of gcorcfcrcncing is preceded by: a. from a vehicle database, retrieving a canonical 3D model rendering of the identified vehicle; b. isolating the damaged parts from the canonical model; c. comparing the images to the isolated undamaged parts obtained from the canonical model; and d. detecting a plurality of key points associated with surface anomalies on the images. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the step of georeferencing comprises: CA 03160583 2022-6-2 WO 2021/113279 PCT/US2020/062775 a. extracting a plurality of stable key points of the anomalies on the at least one image; b. referencing each stable key point on a two-dimensional (2D) coordinate system; c. using the canonical 3D model rendering, calculating geodetic coordinates of each stable key point; and d. geospatially positioning each stable key point onto the at least one damaged part. 7. The method of claim 1, further comprising: a. comparing the generated unique damage print to the damage prints stored on the damage print database; and b. if the generated damage print matches a unique damage print stored on the damage print database, generating a fraud alarm; else c. storing the generated unique damage print in the damage prints database. 8. The method of claim 4; further comprising: a. from the plurality of images, generating a plurality of image pairs; b. for each image pair, estimating the area of vehicle covered in each image in the pair; c. using fragment matching, matching a first image to the second image in the pair for all image pairs determined to have at least one image in the image pair, that has sufficient vehicle area coverage, wherein the image that has sufficient vehicle area coverage is the main pair image and the second image in the pair is a fragment image; d. creating a matched pairs group; e. adding the matched image pairs to the matched pairs group; f. georeferencing the main image in the matched image pair, for each image pairs in the matched pairs group; g. georeferencing the fragment image in the matched image pair, for each image pairs in the matched pairs group. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the step of georeferencing each of the main image in the matched image pair, and each fragment image in the matched image pair is preceded by: a. from a vehicle database, retrieving a canonical 3D model rendering of the identified vehicle; b. isolating the undamaged parts from the canonical model; c. comparing the images to the isolated undamaged parts obtained from the canonical model; and CA 03160583 2022-6-2 WO 2021/113279 PCT7US2020/062775 d. detecting a plurality of points associated with surface anomalies on the images. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the step of gcorcfcrcncing, for each of the main image in the matched image pair, and each fragment image in the matched image pair, comprises a. extracting a plurality of stable key points of the anomalies on the at least one image; b. referencing each stable key point on a two-dimensional (2D) coordinate system; c. using the canonical 3D model rendering, calculating geodetic coordinates of each stable key point; and d. geospatially positioning each stable key point onto the at least one damaged part. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of identifying the vehicle is preceded by: a. determine the presence of a vehicle in the image; and b. if there is no vehicle in the image, generating an alert; else c. identifying the vehicle. 12. Identify at least one damages part in the vehicle; iv. georeference the damage on the identified damaged vehicle part; v. register the georeferenced damage to a 3D model of the identified vehicle; and vi. generate a unique damage print. 17. title: METHOD, DEVICE AND MACHINE FOR CALCULATING AN INDEX REPRESENTATIVE OF THE PROPERTIES OF A MATERIAL OF A MATERIAL OF THE BONE TYPE; PROCEDE, DISPOSITIF ET MACHINE POUR CALCULER UN INDICE REPRESENTATIF DES PROPRIETES DE MATERIAU D'UN MATERIAU DU TYPE OSSEUX
abstract:
Method, device and machine for calculating an index representative of the properties of a material of the bone type of an individual to be subjected to tests, particularly wherein the method comprises a first acquisition step for acquiring at least one image comprising a plurality of elementary units of a sample of the material, wherein a generation step is provided for generating a grid of elementary geometric elements, or cells, which is associated with, in particular superimposed on, said image, an image processing step (12) in which it is provided to calculate at least the apparent elastic modulus (E*) and a density coefficient (C) of the material, both as a function of characteristic values of each cell, and a calculation step (14) for calculating the index representative of the properties of a material, wherein the index is a function of the value of the apparent elastic modulus (E*) net of the contribution of the density coefficient (C).

Procédé, dispositif et machine pour calculer un indice représentatif des propriétés d'un matériau du type osseux d'un individu à soumettre à des essais, en particulier, le procédé comprenant une première étape d'acquisition pour acquérir au moins une image comprenant une pluralité d'unités élémentaires d'un échantillon du matériau, une étape de génération prévue pour générer une grille d'éléments géométriques élémentaires, ou des cellules, qui est associée, en particulier superposée à, ladite image, une étape de traitement d'image (12) dans laquelle il est prévu de calculer au moins le module élastique apparent (E*) et un coefficient de densité (C) du matériau, en fonction de valeurs caractéristiques de chaque cellule, et une étape de calcul (14) pour calculer l'indice représentatif des propriétés d'un matériau, l'indice étant une fonction de la valeur du réseau élastique apparent (E*) de la contribution du coefficient de densité (C).
claims: WO 2021/139870 PCT/EP2020/000220 CLAIMS 1. Method for calculating an index representative of the properties of a monophase or multiphase material, wherein the material is a material of the bone type, of an individual to be subjected to tests, the method comprising: - an acquisition step (10) for acquiring at least one image of a sample of said material, said image allowing to distinguish from each other the one or more phases object of the study inside a closed region of interest (ROI), in which the subsequent processings will be carried out, - a generation step for generating a grid of elementary geometric elements, or cells, which is associated with, in particular superimposed on, said image, - a step in which, for each of the cells covering the region of interest (ROI), it is provided to acquire a parameter (Icell) indicative of the presence or absence in the cell of the phase considered (for example the bone phase): in the event in which the phase is present, said indicative parameter (Icell) is proportional to the quantity of the phase present in the cell, - a processing step (12) for processing the image, in which it is provided to calculate a density coefficient (C) of the material as a function of characteristic values of each cell, calculated on the basis of the value(s) of said parameter (Icell) of the phases falling within the cell; said density coefficient (C) being, for each region of interest (ROI), a univocal value calculated as the sum of said parameters (Icell) involved; and wherein it is provided to calculate the value of the apparent elastic modulus (E*) by means of simulations of the behaviour under load resulting from the application of modes known in the art, in which the mechanical behaviour of the internal structure of the region of interest is simulated, to which each cell contributes with its own value of apparent elastic modulus (E*) and of said parameter (Icell), said method comprising a calculation step (14) for calculating said index (BSI) representative of the properties of a material, wherein said index (BSI) is proportional to the value of the apparent elastic modulus (E*) net of the contribution of said density coefficient (C), said method being characterised in that it comprises a rotation step for rotating said image acquired after said image acquisition step and before said acquisition step for acquiring said parameter (Icell), said rotation step being a rotation step by such a quantity that an orientation of the CA 03163143 2022-6-27 WO 2021/139870 PCT/EP2020/000220 structure of the bone tissue is substantially parallel to Cartesian axes of said elementary geometric elements. 2. Method according to claim 1, characterised in that said index (BSI) is calculated according to the following formula: BSI= f[(fi(E>f 2 (C)] in which f, fi and f 2 are functions that are different from each other, and in particular: f is a function of the exposure parameters used during the first image acquisition step; fi and f 2 are functions whose result is a constantly positive value such that the result of fz is subtracted from fi in such a way as to obtain a subtraction of a contribution due to the density of the material to the contribution due to the elastic modulus. 3. Method according to claim 1, characterised in that said index (BSI) is calculated according to the following formula: BSI = ai(biE* - b 2 C) where; E* is said apparent elastic modulus (apparent Young’s modulus), and C is said density coefficient. ai is a value function of the exposure parameters used during said Image acquisition step (10); bi and b 2 are positive constants, in such a way as to obtain a subtraction of a contribution due to the density of the material to the contribution due to the elastic modulus. 4. Method according to any of the previous claims, characterised in that in said first acquisition step (10) it is provided to acquire three images of samples to be analysed of the individual to be subjected to tests; it being provided to repeat said processing step (12) for each image acquired thus obtaining at least one value of apparent elastic modulus (E*) and one value of density coefficient (C) for each image; a calculation step (13) of the respective mean values being then provided. 5. Method according to any of the previous claims, characterised in that said phase object of the study is formed by a plurality of elementary units (31, 32, 33) defining a three-dimensional matrix of uprights and crosspieces connected to one another, and in that after said acquisition step (10) an image pre-treatment step (12A) is provided in which it is provided to filter said image and to apply CA 03163143 2022-6-27 WO 2021/139870 PCT/EP2020/000220 said rotation step of said image by such a quantity that a direction of extension of said elementary units (31, 32, 33) is substantially parallel to respective horizontal (X) and vertical (Y) directions that are arranged in such a way as to define the Cartesian axes of the plane (X-Y). 6. Method according to claim 5, characterised in that after said image pre-treatment step (12A) a definition step (12B) is provided for defining the region of interest (ROI), defined as the largest square or rectangular area that is fully inscribed in the sample of material being analysed. 7. Method according to the previous claim, characterised in that said processing step (12), said image pre-treatment step (12A), said definition step (12B) for defining the region of interest (ROI), said calculation step (13) for calculating the respective mean values of the apparent elastic modulus (E*) and of the density coefficient (C), and said calculation step (14) for calculating the index (BSI) are automatically performed by means of image processing algorithms, and/or by means of machine learning techniques of the type with neural networks; the machine learning techniques can comprise both neural networks with reinforcement” learning and convolutional neural networks, optionally in conjunction with fuzzy logic techniques. 8. Method according to any of the previous claims, characterised in that said image acquisition step (10) is performed by means of a detection system (1,20) comprising: - X-ray emitting means with which screening means (3,25) are associated, which are configured to screen a user or an operator from the radiation produced by the X-ray emitting means, - sensor means (19, 27), intended to be associated, in use, with the sample of material to be analysed, for example with the chosen anatomical site of interest of the individual to be subjected to tests, in order to detect the X-rays emitted by the X-rays emitting means. 9. Method according to any of claims 8 to 9, characterised in that said image acquisition step (10) is performed by means of a detection system consisting of a portable device (20) provided with a handle (22) for an operator, which is connected to a body (23) within which said X-ray emitting means are arranged and with which said screening means (25) are associated, the detection system further CA 03163143 2022-6-27 WO 2021/139870 PCT/EP2020/000220 comprising a support base (21) configured to support in a stable way said portable device (20), at least during said image acquisition step (10), said sensor means (27) consisting of an X-ray digital sensor built in a flexible band wrapped around one finger of the individual to be subjected to tests, in order to detect the X-rays emitted by the X-ray emitting means. 11. Method according to any of claims 8 to 9, characterised in that said image acquisition step (10) is performed by means of a detection system consisting of a measuring machine (1) provided with a frame (2) comprising screening walls (3) wherein the measuring machine (1) comprises said X-ray emitting means, the measuring machine (1) comprising a support (28) for resting one hand of the individual to be subjected to tests, the measuring machine (1) comprising said sensor means (19, 27) intended to cooperate with the X-ray emitting means to acquire said at least one image of a sample of said material, the sensor means (19,27) being made in the form of a sensor plate (19) for detecting the X-rays emitted by the X-ray emitting means. 12. Detection system (1,20) for detecting images of a sample of material to be analysed suitable to be used by an operator when performing one or more steps of the method according to any of claims 1 to 7, characterised in that said detection system (1, 20) is made in the form of a portable device (20) provided with a handle (22) for the operator, which is connected to a body (23) within which Xray emitting means are arranged and with which screening means (25), configured to screen the operator from the radiation produced by the X-ray means, are associated, a support base (21) configured to support in a stable way said portable device (20), at least during said acquisition step (10) for acquiring the images of the sample of material to be analysed, and sensor means (27), of the digital type, intended to be associated with the sample of material to be analysed, for example with the chosen anatomical site of interest in case of bone material, in order to detect the X-rays emitted by the X-ray emitting means. 13.
