Detection system (1,20) for detecting images of a sample of material to be analysed suitable to CA 03163143 2022-6-27 WO 2021/139870 32 PCT/EP2020/000220 be used by an individual to be subjected to tests if necessary with the help of an operator when performing one or more steps of the method as in any of claims 1 to 7, characterised in that said detection system (1, 20) is made in the form of a measuring machine (1) provided with a frame (2) comprising screening walls (3), wherein the measuring machine (1) comprises said X-ray emitting means, the measuring machine (1) comprising a support (28) for resting one hand of the individual to be subjected to tests, the measuring machine (1 ) comprising said sensor means (19,27) intended to cooperate with the X-ray emitting means to acquire said at least one image of a sample of said material, the sensor means (19, 27) being made in the form of a sensor plate (19) for detecting the X-rays emitted by the X-ray emitting means. 15. Detection system (1,20) according to the previous daim, characterised in that the support (28) comprises hand positioning means selected from: • positioning lines (35) drawn on the support (28) wherein the positioning lines (35) are flat; - light positioning lines (35) lighting up the support (28) by means of backlighting or projection; - positioning lines (35) arranged on the support (28) wherein the positioning lines (35) are raised; - resting surfaces having shapes corresponding to the hand’s shape: - a band for fixing the hand in the optimal position on the support (28). 16. Detection system (1, 20) according to any of claims 13 to 15, characterised in that the support (28) is steady and is positioned inside the measuring machine (1) and said sensor plate (19) for detecting the X-rays is integrated on the surface of said support (28). 17. Detection system (1, 20) according to any of claims 13 to 15, characterised in that the support (28) is fixed to the measuring machine (1) in a sliding way by means of sliding guides (29) in such a way that the support (28) realizes a carriage (17) that enables a movement of the support (28) between a first position that is pulled-out with respect to an insertion slit (5) and a second position of insertion in which the support (28) is almost fully or fully inserted in the measuring machine (1 ). 18. title: MULTIPLE INPUT MACHINE LEARNING FRAMEWORK FOR ANOMALY DETECTION; CADRE D'APPRENTISSAGE AUTOMATIQUE A ENTREES MULTIPLES POUR LA DETECTION D'ANOMALIE
abstract:
A method that includes extracting image features of a document image, executing an optical character recognition (OCR) engine on the document image to obtain OCR output, and extracting OCR features from the OCR output. The method further includes executing an anomaly detection model using features including the OCR features and the image features to generate anomaly score, and presenting anomaly score.

Une méthode comprenant l’extraction de caractéristiques d’une image documentaire, l’exécution d’un moteur de reconnaissance optique de caractères (ROC) sur l’image documentaire dans le but d’obtenir une sortie ROC et l’extraction de caractéristiques ROC de la sortie ROC. La méthode comprend également l’exécution d’un modèle de détection d’anomalies utilisant des caractéristiques y compris les caractéristiques ROC et les caractéristiques de l’image pour générer un score d’anomalie et la présentation du score d’anomalie.

claims: 1. A method comprising: extracting a plurality of image features of a document image; executing an optical character recognition (OCR) engine on the document image to obtain OCR output; extracting a plurality of OCR features from the OCR output; executing an anomaly detection model using a plurality of features comprising the plurality of OCR features and the plurality of image features to generate anomaly score; and presenting anomaly score. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: executing a convolutional neural network-based architecture on the document image to obtain an image embedding model features, wherein the plurality of image features comprises the image embedding model features. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the image embedding model features are output of a fully connected layer of the convolutional neural network. 4. The method of claim 1, further comprising: calculating a plurality of general image features from a plurality of pixels of the document image, wherein the plurality of image features comprises the plurality of general image features. Date Reçue/Date Received 2022-06-21 5. The method of claim 1, further comprising: extracting a plurality of text features using the OCR output and a template of a document type corresponding to the document image, wherein the plurality of features further comprises the plurality of text features. 6. The method of claim 5, further comprising: training a term frequency inverse document frequency (TF-IDF) vectorizer using the template to obtain a trained TF-IDF vectorizer; and executing the trained TF-IDF vectorizer on the OCR output to obtain the plurality of text features. 7. The method of claim 5, wherein the plurality of text features is a vector for each unique keyword in the document with a corresponding frequency of the keyword. 8. The method of claim 1, further comprising: selecting a subset of the plurality of features based on a learned correlation between each of the plurality of features and an oracle accuracy. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising: determining a plurality of weights of the plurality of features; and selecting the subset having a weight above a weight threshold. 10. The method of claim 1, further comprising: executing a variational autoencoder (VAE) model as the anomaly detection model on a subset of the plurality of features to obtain the reconstructed input; and compare the reconstructed input against the subset of the plurality of features to obtain the anomaly score. Date Reçue/Date Received 2022-06-21 11. The method of claim 10, further comprising: comparing the anomaly score against an anomaly threshold; and flagging the document image as anomalous when the extraction accuracy satisfies the anomaly threshold. 12. The method of claim 1, further comprising: identify a training document image; executing an anomaly detection model on a second plurality of features obtained for the training document image to obtain second reconstructed input; determine a reconstruction error based at least in part on the reconstructed input; update, by the anomaly detection model, a plurality of weights of the anomaly detection model using the reconstruction error. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising: for each extraction model of a plurality of extraction models, generating, by the extraction model, a plurality of key identifier, key value pairs for the extraction model for a possible training document image, and comparing the plurality of key identifier, key value pairs for the extraction model with a plurality of key identifier, key value pairs in ground truth information defined for the possible training document image to obtain a combined accuracy level for the extraction model; aggregating the combined accuracy level across the plurality of extraction models to obtain an oracle accuracy for the possible training document image; and selecting the possible training document image as a training document image when the oracle accuracy is greater than an accuracy threshold. Date Reçue/Date Received 2022-06-21 14. The method of claim 1, further comprising: performing, by the anomaly detection model, unsupervised learning using a plurality of training document images. 15. A system comprising: a computer processor; an image feature extractor configured to extract a plurality of image features from a document image; an OCR feature extractor configured to extract a plurality of optical character recognition engine (OCR) features from OCR output of an OCR engine; and an anomaly detection model, executing on the computer processor, configured to generate an anomaly score using a plurality of features comprising the plurality of OCR features and the plurality of image features. 16. The system of claim 15, further comprising an image embedding model trained to classify document images into document types, wherein the image embedding model generates a plurality of image embedding model features of the document image, the plurality of image embedding model features in the plurality of features. 17. The system of claim 15, further comprising: a feature selection model configured to select a subset of features based on relevance to determining the extraction accuracy. 18. The system of claim 15, further comprising: a text feature extractor configured to: extract a plurality of text features using the OCR output and a template of a document type corresponding to the document image, wherein the plurality of features further comprises the plurality of text features. Date Reçue/Date Received 2022-06-21 19. A method comprising: extracting a plurality of image features of a training document image; executing an optical character recognition (OCR) engine on the training document image to obtain OCR output; extracting a plurality of OCR features from the OCR output; executing an anomaly detection model using a plurality of features comprising the plurality of OCR features and the plurality of image features to obtain reconstructed input; determining, by the anomaly detection model, reconstruction error, based at least in part on the reconstructed input; and updating, by the anomaly detection model, a plurality of weights of the anomaly detection model using the reconstruction error. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising: for each extraction model of a plurality of extraction models, generating, by the extraction model, a plurality of key identifier, key value pairs for the extraction model for a possible training document image, and comparing the plurality of key identifier, key value pairs for the extraction model with a plurality of key identifier, key value pairs in ground truth information defined for the possible training document image to obtain a combined accuracy level for the extraction model; aggregating the combined accuracy level across the plurality of extraction models to obtain an oracle accuracy for the possible training document image; and selecting the possible training document image as a training document image when the oracle accuracy is greater than an accuracy threshold.

title: METHOD AND SYSTEM FOR VISUAL INSPECTION OF WIND TURBINE GENERATORS; PROCEDE ET SYSTEME D'INSPECTION VISUELLE DE GENERATEURS EOLIENS
abstract:
Disclosed is a method of visually inspecting a wind turbine generator (WTG) and parts thereof during operation. The method comprising acts of pointing a visual inspection system with a field of view about a line of sight in a plane of rotor blade of the wind turbine generator (WTG); of capturing multiple images of the field of view using the visual inspection system; of selecting at least one reference image amongst the captured images or from elsewhere; of comparing the at least one other captured image with the at least one reference image; and thus inspecting structural aspects of the wind turbine generator (WTG) as a function of the result of the act of comparing.

L'invention concerne un procédé d'inspection visuelle d'un générateur éolien (WTG) et de ses parties pendant le fonctionnement. Le procédé comprend les étapes consistant à : pointer un système d'inspection visuelle avec un champ de vision autour d'une ligne de visée dans un plan de pale de rotor du générateur éolien (WTG) ; capturer de multiples images du champ de vision à l'aide du système d'inspection visuelle ; sélectionner au moins une image de référence parmi les images capturées ou d'ailleurs ; comparer l'au moins une image capturée ailleurs avec l'au moins une image de référence ; et ainsi inspecter les aspects structurels du générateur éolien (WTG) en fonction du résultat de l'action de la comparaison.

title: METHOD AND DEVICE FOR DEPLOYING AND USING AN IMAGE SIMILARITY METRIC WITH DEEP LEARNING; PROCEDE ET DISPOSITIF DESTINE AU DEPLOIEMENT ET D'UTILISATION D'UNE METRIQUE DE SIMILARITE D'IMAGES A APPRENTISSAGE PROFOND
abstract:
The present invention refers to a method and a device that can measure an unknown target coating, can search, based on the measured data of the target coating, a database for one or more best matching coating formulas, i.e. one or more preliminary matching formulas, within the database and that can refine the search using an image similarity metric between images of the one or more best matching coating formulas on the one side and images of the target coating on the other side, using deep learning techniques.

La présente invention concerne un procédé et un dispositif qui peuvent mesurer un revêtement cible inconnu, peuvent rechercher, en fonction des données mesurées du revêtement cible, une ou plusieurs formules du revêtement le mieux apparié dans une base de données, c.-à-d. une ou plusieurs formules d'appariement préliminaires, à l'intérieur de la base de données et qui peuvent affiner la recherche à l'aide d'une mesure de similarité d'images entre des images de la ou des formules du revêtement le mieux apparié sur le premier côté et des images du revêtement cible sur l'autre côté, à l'aide de techniques d'apprentissage profond.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR IMAGING REFLECTING OBJECTS; SYSTEME ET PROCEDE D'IMAGERIE D'OBJETS REFLECHISSANTS
abstract:
A method and system for automated visual inspection of an object include using different patterns of illumination, each pattern including constant frequency pulses of illumination. The pulses of each different pattern of illumination are temporally offset so as to enable obtaining images illuminated by one or another illumination pattern. The images are combined using different parts of the different images in which the most details of the object are available, to produce a full image of the object which is essentially glare-free. The use of constant frequency pulses enables obtaining different pattern illumination images, to enable creating a glare-free image of an object, while providing a flicker- free inspection environment for human workers.

L'invention concerne un procédé et un système d'inspection visuelle automatisée d'un objet, consistant à utiliser différents motifs d'éclairage, chaque motif comprenant des impulsions à fréquence constante d'éclairage. Les impulsions de chaque motif différent d'éclairage sont décalées temporellement de façon à permettre l'obtention d'images éclairées par l'un ou l'autre motif d'éclairage. Les images sont combinées à l'aide de différentes parties des différentes images dans lesquelles la plupart des détails sont disponibles, pour donner une image complète de l'objet qui soit sensiblement exempte d'éblouissement. L'utilisation d'impulsions à fréquence constante permet d'obtenir différentes images des motifs d'éclairage, pour permettre la création d'une image exempte d'éblouissement d'un objet, tout en fournissant un environnement d'inspection sans scintillement pour des travailleurs humains.

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR ASSESSING CELL GROWTH RATES; SYSTEMES ET PROCEDES POUR EVALUER DES TAUX DE CROISSANCE CELLULAIRE
abstract:
A method of facilitating a growth assessment for a cell line includes generating an image of a well that contains a medium that was inoculated with at least one cell of the cell line. The method also includes generating a down-sampled segmentation map comprising pixels that indicate an inferred presence or absence of a cell colony in corresponding portions of the well image. Generating the down-sampled segmentation map includes inputting the well image to a fully convolutional neural network having a plurality of convolutional layers. The method also includes (i) determining, by inputting colony size information (including the down-sampled segmentation map and/or a pixel count derived therefrom) to a cell growth assessment algorithm, a growth classification or score for the cell line, and causing a display of the growth classification or score, and/or (II) causing a display of the colony size information to facilitate a manual cell growth assessment.

Procédé pour faciliter une évaluation de croissance pour une lignée cellulaire comprenant la génération d'une image d'un puits qui contient un milieu qui a été inoculé avec au moins une cellule de la lignée cellulaire. Le procédé comprend également la génération d'une carte de segmentation sous-échantillonnée comprenant des pixels qui indiquent une présence ou une absence inférée d'une colonie de cellules dans des parties correspondantes de l'image de puits. La génération de la carte de segmentation sous-échantillonnée comprend l'entrée de l'image de puits dans un réseau neuronal totalement convolutif ayant une pluralité de couches de convolution. Le procédé comprend également (i) la détermination, par l'entrée d'informations de taille de colonie (comprenant la carte de segmentation sous-échantillonnée et/ou un comptage de pixels dérivé de celle-ci) dans un algorithme d'évaluation de croissance cellulaire, d'une classification ou d'un score de croissance pour la lignée cellulaire, et la réalisation d'un affichage de la classification ou du score de croissance, et/ou (II) la réalisation d'un affichage des informations de taille de colonie pour faciliter une évaluation de croissance de cellule manuelle.

title: IDENTIFICATION OF EFFECT PIGMENTS IN A TARGET COATING; IDENTIFICATION DE PIGMENTS A EFFET DANS UN REVETEMENT CIBLE
abstract:
The present invention refers to a computer-implemented method, the method comprising at least the following steps: 1) providing digital images and respective formulas for coating compositions with known pigments and/or pigment classes associated with the respective digital images, 2) classifying, using an image annotation tool, for each image each pixel, by visually reviewing the respective image pixel-wise, using at least one image segmentation technique and annotating each pixel with a pigment label and/or a pigment class label in alignment with the visual appearance and the formula associated with the respective image, 3) providing, for each image, an associated pixel wise annotated image, 4) training a first neural network (310), implemented and running on at least one computer processor, with the provided digital images as input and the associated pixel-wise annotated images as output wherein the first neural network (310) is trained to classify/correlate every pixel in a respective input image with a pigment label and/or pigment class label of a respective associated annotated image, 5) making the trained first neural network (310) available in the at least one computer processor for applying the trained first neural network to at least one unknown input image of a target coating and for assigning a pigment label and/or a pigment class label to each pixel in the input image, 6) determining and/or outputting, for each input image, based on the assigned pigment labels and/or pigment class labels, a statistic of corresponding identified pigments and/or pigment classes, respectively. The present invention further provides a respective device and a computer-readable medium.

La présente invention concerne un procédé mis en ?uvre par ordinateur, le procédé comprenant au moins les étapes suivantes : 1) la fourniture d'images numériques et de formules respectives pour des compositions de revêtement avec des pigments et/ou des classes de pigments connus associés aux images numériques respectives, 2) la classification, à l'aide d'un outil d'annotation d'image, pour chaque image, de chaque pixel, par examen visuel de l'image respective par pixel, en utilisant au moins une technique de segmentation d'image et en annotant chaque pixel avec une étiquette de pigment et/ou une étiquette de classe de pigment en alignement avec l'aspect visuel et la formule associée à l'image respective, 3) la fourniture, pour chaque image, d'une image annotée par pixel associée, 4) la formation d'un premier réseau neuronal (310), mis en ?uvre et en cours d'exécution sur au moins un processeur informatique, avec les images numériques fournies en tant qu'entrée et les images annotées par pixel associées en tant que sortie, le premier réseau neuronal (310) étant entraîné pour classer/corréler chaque pixel dans une image d'entrée respective avec une étiquette de pigment et/ou une étiquette de classe de pigment d'une image annotée associée respective, 5) la mise à disposition du premier réseau neuronal entraîné (310) dans le ou les processeurs informatiques pour appliquer le premier réseau neuronal entraîné à au moins une image d'entrée inconnue d'un revêtement cible et pour attribuer une étiquette de pigment et/ou une étiquette de classe de pigment à chaque pixel dans l'image d'entrée, 6) la détermination et/ou l'émission, pour chaque image d'entrée, sur la base des étiquettes de pigment et/ou des étiquettes de classe de pigment attribuées, d'une statistique de pigments et/ou de classes de pigment identifiés correspondants, respectivement. La présente invention concerne en outre un dispositif respectif et un support lisible par ordinateur.

title: DEEP LEARNING PLATFORMS FOR AUTOMATED VISUAL INSPECTION; PLATES-FORMES D'APPRENTISSSAGE PROFOND POUR INSPECTION VISUELLE AUTOMATISEE
abstract:
Techniques that facilitate the development and/or modification of an automated visual inspection (AVI) system that implements deep learning are described herein. Some aspects facilitate the generation of a large and diverse training image library, such as by digitally modifying images of real-world containers, and/or generating synthetic container images using a deep generative model. Other aspects decrease the use of processing resources for training, and/or making inferences with, neural networks in an AVI system, such as by automatically reducing the pixel sizes of training images (e.g., by down-sampling and/or selectively cropping container images). Still other aspects facilitate the testing or qualification of an AVI neural network by automatically analyzing a heatmap or bounding box generated by the neural network. Various other techniques are also described herein.

L'invention concerne des techniques qui facilitent le développement et/ou la modification d'un système d'inspection visuelle automatisée (AVI) qui met en uvre un apprentissage profond. Certains aspects facilitent la génération d'une bibliothèque d'images d'apprentissage grande et diverse, par exemple par modification numérique d'images de conteneurs du monde réel, et/ou génération d'images de conteneurs synthétiques à l'aide d'un modèle génératif profond. D'autres aspects réduisent l'utilisation de ressources de traitement pour l'apprentissage et/ou la réalisation d'inférences avec des réseaux de neurones artificiels dans un système d'AVI, par exemple par réduction automatique des tailles de pixels d'images d'apprentissage (par exemple, par sous-échantillonnage et/ou recadrage sélectif d'images de conteneur). D'autres aspects encore facilitent l'essai ou la qualification d'un réseau de neurones artificiels d'AVI par l'analyse automatique d'une carte thermique ou d'un rectangle englobant généré par le réseau de neurones artificiels. Une variété d'autres techniques est également décrite.
